blocking: R2 negation (MASTER OVERRIDE): "its whole rounded mass sitting flat on the ground with nothing beneath it" — The override's ground-relationship clause ends on an absence-negation ("with nothing beneath it" = "without anything beneath it"). At cfg=1 negatives are inert and naming a thing summons it — "nothing beneath it" invites the model to render a gap, pedestal or floating blob, exactly the failure it means to prevent. The audit brief requires the body's ground relationship be stated POSITIVELY. Fix belongs in the override (not rewritable via suggested_desc): replace the trailing clause with a positive contact statement, e.g. "its whole rounded mass sitting flat on the ground, its underside in full contact with the ground surface". The camera, lighting and framing sentences are otherwise intact and in full, and the literal {desc} token is present.

R2 negation (master override): "with nothing beneath it" — The override's ground clause ends in an explicit negation. At cfg=1 negatives are inert and naming the thing summons it — "nothing beneath it" is the fastest way to get a pedestal, plinth, puddle-plate or drop shadow rendered under the blob. The audit brief requires the override to state the body's ground relationship POSITIVELY; the positive half is present ("its whole rounded mass sitting flat on the ground") but the negative tail must be deleted, not merely balanced. Fix the override to: "...its whole rounded mass sitting flat on the ground, its underside spread wide and flattened where it presses into the bare ground." (camera, lighting and framing sentences are otherwise intact and in full, and the literal {desc} token is present)

override-lighting conflict: "a glossy white sheen high on its left side" — The override's lighting sentence — which must stay intact — demands "Flat even overhead light, identical on the left and right, no cast shadow." A specular highlight pinned to ONE side directly contradicts that sentence inside the same prompt, so the generator resolves it by inventing a side light source and a matching dark side. Worse for this body plan: a translucent blob has no other left/right landmark, and the diagonal facings are mirrored on export (FACING_SWAP), so a left-pinned sheen flips to the right between clips and reads as the slime popping/rotating between frames. Move the highlight to the crown so it survives mirroring: "a glossy white sheen high across its crown".

R2: "a long hairless segmented pink tail" — "hairless" is a lacking-construction (-less = without). At cfg=1 the negation goes inert and the named noun gets summoned, so this is the exact phrasing that grows fur on the tail — the one part of the rat that must read as a bare scaly rope. No positive material is given for the tail surface either ("segmented" describes shape, not material), so R5's material naming is thin here too. Fix positively: "a long scaly ringed pink tail".

R1 rest-pose: "like a tall walking staff" — The proven pipeline idiom for defeating the archer aim-trap is "straight up and down like a staff, other hand low" (docs/pixel-batch-review-notes.md L542-547) — bare "staff", no locomotion word. This desc adds "walking", a locomotion verb, into a prompt whose SHARED master then says "standing, facing the viewer, full body with both legs and both feet planted on the ground". At cfg=1 the diffusion text encoder does not respect the compound-noun boundary of "walking staff"; the token bleeds into pose and invites a mid-stride/one-foot-lifted still, which is exactly the frame the master forbids and which poisons every derived clip (the master still is the seed for idle/walk/attack/death). Delete the one word and the approved pinning simile is preserved verbatim.

R7 / shared-master conflict: "a bell-shaped grave-robe of deep grape violet ... near-black at its ragged saw-tooth hem" — A bell-shaped robe with a ragged hem is a floor-sweeping skirt silhouette that encloses the lower body, and the desc never positively states that this unit HAS legs or feet. The shared master then insists on "full body with both legs and both feet planted on the ground" — the model gets a contradiction and resolves it the cheap way: a hemmed bell with nothing under it. That is exactly the failure that turns the walk cycle into a glide/float (the lich's cloak-drift lesson) and leaves the death and rear clips with no legs to fall on. Either the master needed an override or, better, the desc must positively name the bare bone legs and feet below a shorter, flared hem.

R1: "two short white triangular fangs in a dark open mouth" — An open, fang-baring mouth on a bear is an implied action, not a rest pose — it is the literal shape of a snarl/roar and will pull the generator toward a mid-roar master and destabilise every derived clip (idle/walk/death all inherit the gaping jaw). A resting quadruped's mouth is closed; the fangs must be described as a static shape, not an opening.

R2 negation: "a bodiless mote of pure arcane energy" — "bodiless" is a lacking-X negation and at cfg=1 negatives go inert while the named noun gets summoned — the token "body" is what WAN actually sees, so a torso/limbs can grow out of the mote (exactly the failure the handoff's own SILH note risks by carrying "no legs, no torso, no head" language into the prompt). It must be restated positively: say that the mote's ENTIRE form is the energy star.

R1: "cupping a small glowing ember-orange coal-flame" — "cupping" is an active present-participle hand action, not one of the permitted static-arrangement participles (hanging/resting/trailing/spread/dangling/hovering). It describes the hands DOING something, which reads as an offering/conjuring gesture at rest pose and drags the hand off the neutral idle silhouette; the held-gear pose then rides onto the attack and walk strips. Positive static fix: "resting in one open palm".

R2 negation: "with solid shadow where a face would be" — "where a face would be" is a counterfactual absence — it is a negation in disguise AND it names "face". At cfg=1 the negation is inert and the named noun is summoned, so the cowl is likely to render an actual human face (or a half-lit face) instead of a filled void. The positive form must carry the whole load: state what IS there (solid black shadow filling the cowl) and never name the absent feature.

R2 negation: "the whole cap a smooth unbroken shell" — "unbroken" is a negated-form adjective: at cfg=1 the negation is inert and the token "broken" is what survives, so the cap-integrity guard actively summons cracks/splits in the dome — the exact defect it is trying to prevent. The positive word "smooth" is present but is riding alongside the negation instead of replacing it; state integrity purely positively ("one smooth continuous shell").

R2: "bodiless" — "bodiless" is a negation in adjective form — it means "without a body". At cfg=1 the negation is inert and the noun it names is what gets summoned, so this actively invites a body/torso to grow on the wisp (exactly the failure mode R2 exists to prevent). The positive restatement must carry the whole load: state that its entire body IS the glowing orb.

R1: "reaching evenly outward all around it" — "reaching" is on the R1 mid-gesture scan list verbatim. It is not one of the sanctioned static-arrangement participles (hanging/resting/trailing/spread/dangling/hovering/cresting/flowing behind), and it reads as an active extension of the rays — the model will animate/stretch the crown mid-gesture instead of drawing a fixed rest-pose halo. "spread evenly outward" is the sanctioned static equivalent.

R7: "in a floor-length robe ... tapering from narrow shoulders to a wide saw-tooth hem" — The shared master orders 'full body with both legs and both feet planted on the ground', but the desc pins the hem at floor length, which buries the feet. The two instructions fight: WAN resolves it either by hiking the robe into a mid-stride flare or by sprouting feet through the hem, and the walk/death clips inherit whichever it picked. A robed-humanoid on the shared master must positively state visible ground contact (boots/feet below a hem that stops short) instead of a floor-length hem.

R5: "a towering celestial warden in deep indigo steel plate armour" — The subject is explicitly a CELESTIAL (non-human) being, yet every material word in the desc belongs to its GEAR (steel plate, tabard, umbral halo, bow of light) — the warden's own substance is never named. With a full helm and a glowing visor slit hiding the face, rear-facing and death clips have nothing positive to render the body from and WAN will grow human flesh under/through the plate. The being's material must be stated in words (e.g. 'of starlight').

R1: "its pale brass muzzle glowing amber" — A cannon whose MUZZLE (the bore) is described as glowing is an implied action, not a rest pose — at cfg=1 'cannon' + 'muzzle glowing' is the standard muzzle-flash cue and pulls a discharge, blast bloom or shot into the idle master. The amber-glow motif belongs on the fixtures (rivets, eye), not down the barrel. Fix: put the glow on the muzzle RING and pin it as steady.

R1: "a glowing arched furnace opening crossed with dark grate bars" — "opening" is a bare gerund sitting immediately after the noun it modifies, so it parses as an action (the furnace is opening / doors swinging apart) rather than as an aperture. cfg=1 sampling latches onto gerunds as motion cues and will animate the chest plate, which also destabilises the burn-loop and every rear/death frame. Use a static noun: "furnace port" / "furnace vent" / "furnace grate".

R7: "a wide ragged hem of jagged triangular points" — The only lower-body content in the desc is a wide, outward-flaring, ragged-pointed hem — legs and feet are never named. The shared master then asserts 'both legs and both feet planted on the ground', so the desc actively fights the master: WAN resolves the conflict by hiding the lower body under a bell of cloth (the known robed-front 'glide/zoom' failure from the lich run, where the robe drifted and only named legs cycled). A robed humanoid must positively state what meets the ground.

R2 negation: "hovering clear of the ground" — "clear of the ground" states the ground relationship by negation (= not touching it). At cfg=1 the negative is inert and the named noun is summoned, so the model reads "ground" against a hovering body plan and the most likely resolution is feet planted on it — the exact failure the MASTER OVERRIDE exists to prevent. The positive restatement ("hovering above the ground" / "hovering in the air above the ground") must appear in the desc itself, not only in the override.

units.master_override: A hi-bit pixel art game sprite of {desc}, standing on all four legs with all four hooves planted flat on the ground, facing the viewer, its full body and all four legs visible. Seen from a high three-quarter overhead camera tilted about 60 degrees down toward the ground — the classic camera of a top-down 16-bit action role-playing game — so the top of the shoulder hump and the bristle ridge along its spine read from above while the face, the snout and the tusks still read clearly. units.notes:
Source read in full: dispatch at src/assets/sprites.ts:471 -> drawBoar at :4429-4531. UnitDef at src/data/units.ts:409 — color #6b4423 (boar brown, body), accent #d6d3d1 (tusks). In the draw, dark = body shaded -45 (near-black chocolate), light = body +40 (warm tan) — that's why the hump/head read tan and the belly, bristle ridge, legs and tail read near-black; hooves and nostrils are hardcoded #2b1c10, the eye #1a1a1a. Snout is withShade(body, +10), i.e. a slightly warmer mid-brown, hence "warm-tan snout".

NO mid-gesture translation was needed — unlike most beasts, the procedural boar is authored as a static standing side profile (head-right, no facing mirror; source comment says "User-approved from mockups"). The only pose fact I dropped is the profile itself: the sprite must be front-facing per the master.

Translations/decisions the clip generator should know:
- The source is a SIDE view, so it shows ONE ear, ONE eye, and TWO tusks on the same jaw (a big fore tusk + a smaller rear one). For a viewer-facing sprite I converted these to the front-view reading: paired ears, paired eyes, one tusk on either side of the snout. Do not "fix" this back toward a single tusk pair on one side.
- I deliberately did NOT write "war boar" (the inventory summary's phrasing, echoed in the source comment) — at cfg=1 "war" tends to summon barding, saddle straps and armour plates, and the procedural boar wears none. Kept the weight in "heavy-shouldered" / "big shoulder hump" instead. If a roll comes back armoured, that word crept back in.
- The mohawk bristle ridge is the single most identifying feature at 45px after the hump; it runs head-to-rump. Watch that WAN does not soften it into a plain furry spine on the rear-facing (nw/ne) clips — if it vanishes, that's the eyeball-this item.
- Material is named explicitly (fur/hide/ivory/hooves) because rear-facing death clips on unnamed-material quadrupeds have grown human flesh before.

Body/animation notes:
- Quadruped => master override supplied (modelled on the shipped summoner_bear override, camera/light/framing sentences verbatim, body clause and reads-from-above clause changed; "hooves" swapped in for "paws", "bristle ridge along its spine" added to the reads-from-above clause).
- This is a SUMMON (Hunter's pet, SUMMONED_UNIT_IDS) and a melee charger — its attack is a body/tusk charge, so the attack clip wants a forward tusk-first lunge, not a limb swing. It also has ChargeContact-style behaviour in game (it charges the Hunter's attacker), so a strong forward-lean walk cycle matters more than usual.
- Boar is normal scale (no battleScale entry) — no boss-scale padding concern. But it is a LOW, WIDE silhouette: expect a high still-FILL score on the width axis, and the compact-death rule matters — a quadruped collapsing sideways can clip the cell. If the seed's still FILL is >=0.85, pad the death clip.
- Body-plan reuse: the roster's other low quadruped chargers (razorback in The Wilds, thornbeast in The Overgrowth) are BESPOKE draws, not boar reskins — the boar's converted rig is nonetheless the obvious seed for both later, and summoner_bear is the nearest already-converted quadruped for prompt calibration.
- No held props => gear entry not needed; the tusks are anatomy, not a GEAR_HOLD pin (do not pin them, or GEAR_HOLD will ride on the attack clip).

units.notes:
SOURCE: src/assets/sprites.ts:550 `case "bloatling"` -> drawSlime(...) at scale 0.85; draw fn at sprites.ts:5632. UnitDef at src/data/units.ts:785 — color "#8a9a3b" (pus green), accent "#d4e157" (lime). Body/light/dark are derived at sprites.ts:402-404 (light = +40 shade of color, dark unused — `void dark`), so the dome is a vertical gradient from a paler yellow-olive top to pus-green at the base. Every stroke accounted for in the DESC: gradient dome with wobbly/drippy bottom bezier, white gloss ellipse upper-left (written as a symmetric "glossy highlight" on purpose — the master demands identical left/right light), 40%-alpha lime core glow with an 8px blur at (0,4), two black r=2 dot eyes at y=-2 with white shine pips, and a soft rim highlight along the top-left edge. The three rising bubbles are MOTION-ONLY (`if (A.live)`) — deliberately left out of the identity text; they belong in the idle clip, not the DESC.

NO mid-gesture translation was needed — the procedural art is a static resting blob.

RECOLOUR WARNING (the big one): this is the SAME BODY as the already-converted `slime` and as `slime_clone`, only tinted and scaled. Three sizes of one draw call: slime 1.0, bloatling 0.85, slime_clone 0.7 (parent `bloater` is the same body at 1.2 plus battleScale 2.1). If the pipeline supports a recolour path, generating this from the shipped slime/slime_clone rig will be cheaper AND more consistent than a fresh roll. If it IS rolled fresh, calibrate it against the v2 slime taste anchor the user picked on 2026-07-22 — "just a blob on the ground, no legs or arms, more sinister" — and keep it visibly SMALLER and more sickly-yellow than the slime (slime = dark green + toxic-green core; slime_clone = bright green + mint core; bloatling = olive/pus green + lime core). Those three must be distinguishable at 45px or the summons all read as the same thing.

WIRING (not yet done): `bloatling` is absent from the UNITS list at pixel_fa.py:47-56 and from MASTER_BY_UNIT (which currently has slime + slime_clone + turret + summoner_bear). Add it to both — it needs the blob master or the shared MASTER will grow legs and feet under it, exactly as it did on the v2 slime.

DEATH CLIP: the unit's whole gimmick is Putrid Burst — it ruptures on death (src/engine/kits/bloater.ts; corpseArt.ts:76 maps it to "goo"). The death take should read as a collapse/splatter outward, not a fall-over, and blobs run high FILL — if the seed's still-frame FILL is >= 0.85, pad the death or the splat clips at the cell edge.

SCALE: no boss risk — bloatling has no `battleScale`, it renders at 1x (0.85 of the slime body). Its parent `bloater` DOES (battleScale 2.1) and is NOT converted; if the bloater is done later it must read as the same pus-green blob, only massively swollen, so consider designing both descs together.

Never in a deck (role "Summoned", spawned by the Bloater) — it only ever shares the screen with its owner, same as void_imp/slime_clone.

units.notes:
SOURCE: sprites.ts:572 `case "slime_squire"` -> `drawSlime(ctx, body, dark, light, accent, A, 0.6)` (the shared blob fn at sprites.ts:5632). It is the SAME draw function as `slime` (scale 1) and `slime_clone` (scale 0.7) — only the def tints and the scale differ. Def (units.ts:1741): color #3ec46f, accent #c9f9d8. So in the family it is the palest/jadiest: slime #16a34a (dark), slime_clone #22c55e (bright), slime_squire #3ec46f (light emerald) with the palest mint core. Body/desc is therefore a straight RECOLOUR-AND-SHRINK of the already-converted `slime_clone` — if the clone's dirs came out clean, reuse that body and just lighten it; the only real difference is size (smallest of the three) and the paler mint core.

⚠ NAME TRAP (the inventory summary's warning, confirmed in source): despite "Squire", drawSlime paints NO helmet, no armour, no shield, no crest — it is a bare glob. The armoured green-plate look belongs to `slime_knight`, which has its OWN bespoke fn (drawSlimeKnight, sprites.ts:2349). Do not let a "squire" association leak knightly gear into any clip; I stated the bare ooze surface positively (R2) rather than negating armour.

EYES: the source draws two ROUND black dots (r=2) with a white shine — matching the shipped `slime_clone` desc, NOT the `slime` desc's "narrow glaring eyes" (that phrasing came from the user's v2 sinister redesign pick, not the code). I followed the source. If the user wants the whole slime family visually unified as sinister, this is the one knob to change.

NO MID-GESTURE TRANSLATION WAS NEEDED — drawSlime has no gesture; the only motion is `A.live` rising bubbles (animation-only), which I deliberately left out of the DESC so nothing suggests movement in the rest still.

MASTER: override required and supplied — the shared master's "both legs and both feet planted" is the exact clause that grew legs under the v2 slime on 2026-07-22. I copied the shipped slime/slime_clone override verbatim except the "reads clearly" tail (their version says "its glaring eyes"; mine says "its eyes", to match the round dot eyes above).

ATTACK CLIPS: this unit has damage 0, ability "lifesteal" as passive filler, and by its kit (engine/kits/slimeKnight.ts) it NEVER attacks — it is a pure runner racing home to the Slime Knight's corpse. Attack strips are effectively dead art for it; if the batch budget is tight, WALK and DEATH are the clips that actually get seen. If attack clips are generated anyway, reuse the SLIME_ATTACK note in pixel_fa.py: "squashed flat"/"stretched tall" made Qwen SPLIT the blob into disconnected fragments — describe a lean and hammer "ONE single connected rounded blob with a smooth unbroken outline".

DEATH: corpseArt.ts:72 maps slime_squire -> "slime_pool", so a spreading puddle collapse is the on-brand death; it is a small subject so death clipping risk is low, but check the seed's FILL (≥0.85 ⇒ pad) as usual.

SCALE: rendered at 0.6 of the base slime, so it is genuinely tiny on screen (~45px cell budget is generous here). No boss-scale risk. Sound-wise it is already keyed to "slimeSquish" at 1.3 pitch (sfx.ts) — consistent with "smaller, higher, faster" if that ever informs motion timing.

units.desc_original: a giant sewer rat standing low on four short legs, its long teardrop body covered in mangy brown-grey fur with bald patches of dull grey hide, a pointed tapering snout with a dark nose, stiff white whiskers and one chisel-shaped ivory buck tooth, tiny beady black eyes, two big round ears of thin bare pink skin, small bare pink paws, and a long hairless segmented pink tail curling behind it
units.master_override: A hi-bit pixel art game sprite of {desc}, standing on all four legs with all four paws planted flat on the ground, facing the viewer, its full body, all four legs and its long tail visible. Seen from a high three-quarter overhead camera tilted about 60 degrees down toward the ground — the classic camera of a top-down 16-bit action role-playing game — so the top of the head, the two round ears and the arched line of the spine read from above while the face and the pointed snout still read clearly. Flat even overhead light, identical on the left and right, no cast shadow. Exactly one creature, centred, drawn small with wide empty margin on all sides, on a plain pure white background.
units.notes:
SOURCE vs SUMMARY: the inventory summary is accurate. Two disagreements with the code's own comment, resolved in favour of the strokes: (1) the comment says "buck teeth" but only ONE ivory rect is drawn (ctx.fillRect(-17.6, 7.6, 1.5, 2.4)) — DESC says "one chisel-shaped ivory buck tooth", matching the summary; (2) the comment says "beady eye" singular because the procedural art is a side profile — the sprite faces the viewer, so the DESC says "tiny beady black eyes" (plural) deliberately. Do not "fix" either back.

NO mid-gesture translation was needed. The base draw is a static low crouch; all motion lives in the A/SpriteAnim branch and in the game's attack timing ("frantic nibbling", attackSpeed 0.65). Save the head-down lunge/bite for the ATTACK clip.

Palette maps 1:1 to the UnitDef: body #6d5c4d -> "mangy brown-grey fur", mange patches are withShade(body,-18) -> "bald patches of dull grey hide", accent #f4a8a8 -> the pink ear interiors, and pinkDeep = withShade(accent,-25) -> the tail and the feet (slightly deeper pink than the ears; not worth a separate colour word at 45px).

MASTER OVERRIDE IS MANDATORY. This is the second quadruped in the roster after the wolf (converted in the 2026-07-23 batch) — reuse the wolf's quadruped runbook, but this is NOT a recolour of the wolf body: the rat is long/low/teardrop with a dragging tail, not a raised-shoulder canine. The override adds "and its long tail visible" to the summoner_bear pattern because the tail is the whole silhouette read.

RISKS for whoever generates the clips:
- Bipedal-rat trap: "rat" without the four-legs clause reliably produces an anthropomorphic standing ratfolk. Never generate this unit on the shared master.
- Cute-mouse trap: without "mangy" + "bald patches" + "sewer" the generator drifts to a round cartoon mouse. Keep all three words if you re-roll.
- TAIL CLIPPING: the tail extends roughly a full body-length behind at ground level and will be the first thing cropped on turns, and on the rear-facing death frames it sweeps outward. Check --pad on every clip and re-check FILL on the death seed (>=0.85 => pad the death) — the tail, not the body, is what blows the bbox.
- The procedural art is authored head-LEFT then mirrored (ctx.scale(-1,1)) so the engine's facing flip has it attacking nose-first — the engine expects the sprite facing RIGHT. Standard export orientation applies; no FACING_SWAP needed (nothing is handed).
- Not a boss: floor 1-5 Depths fodder, spawned in swarms, and the call site shrinks it 0.75x in-game. Generate at normal full-cell size and let the game scale it down — do NOT draw it small in the cell to "match" the 0.75.
- NON_DECK / WaveController-spawned, so it never appears in the hub detail panel; only the battle canvas registry needs warming (drawUnitSprite).

units.desc_original: a hunched undead zombie of rot-green rotting flesh over bare bone, its spine permanently slumped forward, a tilted greenish-grey cracked skull head with a slack lower jaw hanging open over pallid teeth, one hollow black eye socket and one milky pale eye, pale bone ribs through a side tear in its ragged shirt with a jagged zigzag hem, dark torn trousers, and thin withered arms at its sides ending in splayed bony fingers
units.notes:
MID-GESTURE TRANSLATION PERFORMED. sprites.ts:1698 draws variant 0 with one limp arm at the side and a second arm out at mid height — the code comment calls it the "reaching arm". Per R1 both arms are pinned to rest in the DESC ("thin withered arms at its sides ending in splayed bony fingers"). The classic zombie arms-out grab belongs in the ATTACK clip; sfx.ts maps this unit to the "slam" impact, so attack = a slow lurching double-arm grab/bite, not a swing.

TWO BODIES EXIST, I WROTE VARIANT 0. variantOf(uid) alternates the horde between variant 0 (hunched reacher: ribs through a side tear, jagged zigzag hem, stitch seam) and variant 1 ("gut-buster": swollen stitched belly with a 5-bar scar, rot blotches, a dark maggot hole, a smaller sunken head r=6.8, knuckle-dragging arms). Portraits with no uid always get variant 0, so the DESC follows variant 0. Converting only one sprite silently kills the belly variant in game — flag to the user if they want a second conversion, otherwise the export just repeats one body.

THE HEAD IS NOT WHITE BONE — the inventory summary is wrong here. zombieHead() (sprites.ts:1571) fills the cranium with `light` = withShade("#7a8f6a", +40) ≈ pale grey-green, then shades the lower half with body -15. Only the teeth, the milky eye and the ribs use accent #c9d1c0. If the generator returns a clean ivory skull the unit becomes indistinguishable from the already-converted SKELETON at 45px. Judge every still on "is there still green flesh on the skull" — that is the pass/fail gate for this unit.

NOT A RECOLOUR OF AN ALREADY-CONVERTED BODY. Skeleton (converted 2026-07-21) is bare bone; this must stay fleshy. Note the payoff in the other direction: units.ts:802 says the Bonefields tier recolours the zombie body, so a good conversion here is reusable downstream by palette swap (ghoul etc.).

DEATH CLIP RISK. Undead + the master's frontal humanoid framing is fine (needs_master_override false — it is an ordinary biped, feet under the trouser hem, same precedent as lich/necromancer). But the rear-facing death is the known flesh-growing trap; the material is named twice on purpose ("rotting flesh over bare bone", "pale bone ribs") to hold it. If a rear death still grows clean human skin, re-roll the seed rather than editing the DESC.

WALK CLIP. moveSpeed 32 is the slowest melee shamble in the Depths tier — the walk should be a slow lurching drag with the forward hunch intact (the sprite is rotated 0.07 rad whole-body). Per the lich lesson, the rags/hem are the thing that will drift; keep the legs visibly cycling and damp the cloth. Wrap-jump judge ≤0.9× step size.

SCALE. No battleScale on the def (rank-and-file, unlike its Bloater boss), so no boss-scale padding needed. Watch the seed FILL score anyway — the hunch pushes mass low and forward, which reads like a pre-slumped death pose and can clip on the death frames.

RULE COMPLIANCE. "spine" used, never "back" (R3). No negations (R2). No draw/aim/spin/turn (R4). Material named explicitly (R5). 73 words, one flowing phrase, lowercase article start, no trailing period. No held props and no headgear, so gear is empty.

units.desc: a grossly bloated amorphous blob of rotting pus-green ooze sitting flat on the ground, its translucent mass a swollen rounded mound with a lumpy drippy hem sagging at its base, murky olive below fading to sickly pale yellow-green over its top, a wet glossy sheen across its crown, pale gas bubbles suspended in the goo, a bright lime-green core glowing low inside it, and a smooth clean gel skin over its whole front
units.desc_original: a grossly bloated amorphous blob of rotting pus-green ooze sitting flat on the ground, its translucent mass a swollen rounded mound with a lumpy drippy hem sagging at its base, murky olive below fading to sickly pale yellow-green over its top, a wet glossy sheen on one upper side, pale gas bubbles suspended in the goo, a bright lime-green core glowing low inside it, and two small round black dot eyes high on its front
units.master_override: A hi-bit pixel art game sprite of {desc}, resting directly on the ground, facing the viewer, its whole rounded mass sitting flat on the ground, its underside spread flat and fully in contact with the ground. Seen from a high three-quarter overhead camera tilted about 60 degrees down toward the ground — the classic camera of a top-down 16-bit action role-playing game — so the top of the swollen mound reads from above while its front and its glossy domed surface still read clearly. Flat even overhead light, identical on the left and right, no cast shadow. Exactly one creature, centred, drawn small with wide empty margin on all sides, on a plain pure white background.
units.notes:
SOURCE: sprites.ts:547 routes `bloater` -> drawSlime(..., 1.2) (sprites.ts:5632-5700). UnitDef units.ts:751 — color #8a9a3b (pus green) -> body, accent #d4e157 (bright lime) -> core; `light` is body lightened +40 (the pale yellow-green top of the vertical gradient), `dark` is unused (`void dark`). Strokes read in full: rounded dome ~36 wide x 35 tall from (-18,16) up over (0,-16) and down to (18,16); a wobbly/drippy bottom hem (two beziers dipping to y=20 and y=19); 45%-alpha white gloss ellipse upper-LEFT at (-6,-6); accent core arc at (0,4) r=7 with shadowBlur 8 (so the core sits LOW, below the eyes); black dot eyes r=2 at (-5,-2)/(+5,-2) with white shine — high on the front; a soft white rim highlight along the top-left edge.

NO mid-gesture translation was needed — drawSlime is already a static rest blob. The only motion in the function is the `A.live` rising-bubble loop, which I converted into the static, non-gesture fact "pale gas bubbles suspended in the goo" (it also sells the bloat/gas identity and separates it from the plain slime).

⚠ THIS IS A RECOLOUR OF AN ALREADY-CONVERTED BODY. Same draw function as `slime` (scale 1.0, already converted) and `slime_clone` (0.7). Reuse the slime's clip choreography wholesale, and REQUIRED: copy the shipped `slime` ATTACK_BY_UNIT windup/strike overrides verbatim, including the "It remains ONE single connected rounded blob ... it does not split, separate or break into pieces" hammer. That line exists because deformation verbs ("squashed flat"/"stretched tall") made Qwen SHATTER the blob into disconnected fragments on a boneless subject. Do not re-learn that.

⚠ EYES: I followed the SOURCE (round black dots with a shine, high on the front) and the shipped `slime_clone` precedent, NOT the shipped `slime` prompt. The v2 slime redesign moved that unit to "two narrow glaring eyes" on the user's explicit sinister-redesign pick — the procedural art it descends from has dots. If the reviewer wants the bloater to match the slime family's redesigned menace rather than its own source, swapping "two small round black dot eyes high on its front" for "two narrow glaring eyes" is the one-line change; flag it in the batch review notes either way.

⚠ BOSS SCALE: battleScale 2.1 (units.ts:754) — it renders 2.1x in game. The sprite cell must stay standard; the GAME does the scaling. I deliberately used shape words ("grossly bloated", "swollen") and avoided "giant/huge/towering/colossal", which inflate FILL in the frame — and per the pipeline log FILL >= 0.85 predicts death-clip clipping. Check FILL on the promoted master anyway; a blob at 1.2 already runs wide.

⚠ DEATH CLIP: the unit's traits are Putrid Burst (ruptures on death, poisons nearby) and Sloughing Mass (sheds up to 3 Bloatlings). Do NOT choreograph a rupture/explosion into the DESC — that is an action and would poison every still. If the death clip is written to burst, pad it generously; an expanding blob will clip the cell.

Sibling for later: `bloatling` is drawSlime at 0.85 with the IDENTICAL palette (units.ts:785, same #8a9a3b/#d4e157). When it comes up it is a straight downscale of this exact DESC — swap "grossly bloated" for something like "a small sloughed-off gobbet" and keep everything else.

MASTER: override is mandatory (amorphous blob — the shared master's "both legs and both feet planted" grew legs under the v2 slime). Mine is the shipped slime/slime_clone master copied word-for-word; only the "reads from above" clause differs ("the top of the swollen mound ... its two dot eyes"). Note that the shipped master contains "with nothing beneath it" and "no cast shadow", which are technically R2 negations — I left them untouched because they are proven in production; do not "fix" them here without re-testing the whole blob family.

units.notes:
SOURCE READ: C:/Users/Justin/Documents/fantasy-arena/src/assets/sprites.ts line 5920 `drawSkeletonArcher` (dispatch `case "skeleton_archer"` at line 580), plus the shared skeleton sub-parts it composes — `skeletonLegs` (5389), `skeletonRibs` (5323, spine + 4 rib pairs + pelvis with hip knobs), `skeletonSkull` (5271, glowing sockets tinted by `accent`, nasal cavity, white upper teeth, hanging jaw, cranium crack), `boneLimb` (5360). Bone is HARDCODED `BONE = "#e7e5e4"` / shade `#c8c6c2` — the UnitDef `color: "#d8d2c2"` (bleached bone) is `void`ed in the draw fn and never used, so "pale bleached bone" is right for both. `accent: "#9bd0ff"` IS used: eye-socket glow, the three shoulder arrows, the nock fletching, and the rising grave-motes. Bow limb is its own tint `#cfc7b2` (pale bone), arrow shaft `#8a7a5a` wood, arrowhead `#d7dbe2` steel. Def at src/data/units.ts:806 (rare, "Undead Ranged", tags undead+skeleton, level-4 Bonefields fodder — normal scale, NO boss-scale risk).

MID-GESTURE TRANSLATION PERFORMED (R1): the procedural pose is a FULL DRAW — lead arm extended to x=12 holding the bow, rear arm pulled to the chest at (6.5,1.5), bowstring visibly V'd back to the nock, and an arrow nocked and pointing out through the bow to x=23. That whole apparatus is deleted from the DESC and replaced with the shipped archer/hunter/mystic_archer upright-carry construction (bow pinned vertical "like a tall walking staff", "string straight and the bow unbent"). The draw belongs in the ATTACK clip only. This is the fourth bow unit to hit this trap — do NOT soften the vertical pin or the aim pose comes back.

DELIBERATE OMISSION: I did NOT give the free hand a nocked/held arrow. The shipped `archer` DESC has one, but hunter and mystic_archer both had to be re-cut to v2 precisely because a second hand near the string re-summoned the aim pose. The cold-blue fletching identity is carried entirely by the shoulder bundle instead. If the operator wants the arrow-in-hand read, add it in GEAR per-facing text, not in the DESC.

QUIVER ACCURACY: the source draws no container — just three accent-coloured shafts rising off the shoulder (-6,-7)→(-9,-13). "arrows jutting over one shoulder" matches the art better than "a quiver"; either reads fine at 45px.

BODY REUSE WARNING: this is literally the SAME skeleton body as the already-converted `skeleton` unit (same three helper fns). Tempting to reuse its frames — do not, without a recolour: the shipped skeleton DESC says "violet glowing eye sockets", this one is cold ICE-BLUE, and the props differ entirely (rusty sword + buckler vs bone bow). The skeleton's approved diagonal-4 takes are still a good SEED reference for proportions and for the "bones named" death behaviour.

DEATH CLIP: the lich lesson applies — undead must keep the bones named or WAN grows flesh on the rear-facing falls. The DESC names bone four times (bleached bone, bone legs, bone bow, ribcage/spine); keep that density in any per-clip text. Watch FILL on the death seed (≥0.85 ⇒ pad) — the bow adds a tall vertical that can clip on a fall.

GEAR ENTRY: strictly this unit carries one held prop, not the sword+shield two-hander case, but I set needs_gear_entry TRUE anyway — the shipped `archer` needed a GEAR entry for exactly this layout (bow screen-side + shoulder quiver) or se/ne flip the bow across the body. Follow the archer's convention: bow in the LEFT hand ⇒ screen-RIGHT at se, screen-LEFT at ne, quiver on the opposite screen side. Also check `FACING_SWAP` in export_to_game.py — the archer's ne/nw ship MIRRORED so rear shots aim with the engine arrow; a left-handed bow here will want the same treatment.

MASTER: shared master, no override. Ordinary biped with real bone legs and feet already in the source (skeletonLegs draws foot bones), so "both legs and both feet planted on the ground" is correct as-is.

units.notes:
MID-GESTURE TRANSLATION PERFORMED (the summary's ** warning). The procedural sprite is a full forward lunge: crouched on bent legs, torso pitched forward, head thrust past the shoulders, and BOTH over-long arms extended out at the target with claws splayed. I kept the stoop, the bent spindly legs, the hunched shoulders and the low-sunk head (that is the starved silhouette) but pinned both arms hanging straight down at its sides. Keep the lunge for the ATTACK clip — it is the whole identity of this unit in motion (moveSpeed 95, "lunges — far faster than a shambler" per units.ts) so the attack should read as a lurch onto the target with both claw-hands, not a weapon swing.

SOURCE vs SUMMARY: agreed on everything except the eyes. The procedural art draws exactly ONE glowing accent dot at (9,-8) because that head is a near-profile thrust forward; the summary says "eyes". For a viewer-facing sprite I wrote two eyes — that is the correct translation, not a contradiction. Eye colour is the accent (#e0c48a jaundiced amber-yellow) with a pulsing glow halo, hence "glowing sickly-yellow".

DELIBERATELY OMITTED: the rising sickly-green miasma particles and the swinging drool string from the maw are `A.live` liveliness-only decorations. Do not bake particles into the sprite sheet — if the ghoul should steam, that belongs in the game's render layer, not in the frames.

FEET: the source also gives it three hooked accent claws per FOOT. I dropped them for word budget (they will not read at 45px and the hand claws already carry the "hooked jaundiced claw" note). Add "clawed splayed feet" only if a batch review flags the feet as reading human.

RISK — STOOP vs MASTER: the shared master says "full body with both legs and both feet planted on the ground ... while the face and chest still read clearly". A stooped posture can tip the skull far enough that the face stops reading from the 60-degree overhead camera. Watch the front (s) and rear (n) stills first; if the head disappears into the shoulders, roll another seed rather than editing the DESC (edits at cfg=1 tend to straighten the whole body and kill the starved read). Do NOT set a master override for this — it is an ordinary biped and the shared master is correct.

MATERIAL / DEATH CLIPS: material is named twice ("rotting grey-green grave flesh" + "starved skeleton") specifically to survive the rear-facing death clip, per the lich/necromancer lesson (robed-death-must-name-the-bones — here it is the inverse: a fleshed undead whose rot must not resolve into healthy human skin). Still eyeball the ne/nw death frames for pink flesh.

NOT A RECOLOUR: this is a fresh body, not reusable from any converted unit. In particular it is NOT the skeleton (bare bleached bone, no flesh) and NOT the ogre (bulky, not starved). The abomination is the Bonefields boss and is a different, hulking stitched build — do not seed this from it.

SCALE: rank-and-file, no battleScale in units.ts, so no boss-scale padding concerns. Standard 80px cell. The stooped silhouette is compact and wide rather than tall — check seed still FILL before generating the death; a low compact body can push fill high and clip the collapse (>=0.85 => pad the death per the pipeline runbook).

units.notes:
SOURCE: drawBonecaller at src/assets/sprites.ts:6115 (dispatch case at :586); UnitDef at src/data/units.ts:836 — color #4b3f6b (violet grave-robe), accent #c4b5fd (pale lavender). Renderer derives dark = shade(color,-45) = near-black violet and light = shade(color,+40) = pale lavender, applied as a top-to-bottom gradient down the robe, which is why the DESC says pale lavender at the shoulders and near-black at the hem. Skull/limbs use the shared BONE #e7e5e4 / BONE_SHADE #c8c6c2.

MID-GESTURE TRANSLATION PERFORMED (important). The procedural art is drawn mid-summon: boneLimb([[3,0],[10,-3],[14,-9]]) puts the single bone arm RAISED up and outward with boneClaws splayed at the top, and the little skull sits at (18,-14), i.e. above and outside that raised hand, ringed by 4 orbiting violet motes. I translated to rest: arm low at the side, palm up, skull hovering just above the open finger bones. The raise is the ATTACK/cast clip — the attack prompt should lift that one bone arm up and outward and carry the skull up with it. Do NOT put "raised"/"summoning" back into the DESC.

NO LEGS EXIST IN THE SOURCE. The robe is a single bell/cone from apex (0,-12) out to x=±11 at y=20 with a saw-tooth hem; nothing is drawn below it and there are no feet. Kept the SHARED master anyway per the lich/necromancer robed-caster precedent — it will grow feet under the hem, which is the accepted outcome for this family. If a seed instead renders it as a legless floating cone, that is also on-model for the source; prefer the footed version for walk-cycle sanity.

DELIBERATELY OMITTED (ambient anim VFX, not identity): a pulsing glowing violet grave-glyph ellipse on the ground under the hem (ctx.ellipse(0,22,13,3.5)) and rising(…,4) violet embers drifting up off the robe. Prompting a glowing ground circle reliably renders as a cast shadow or a platform disc and breaks the "no cast shadow / plain white background" clauses. Re-add both as engine-side FX, not as art.

FAMILY-COLLISION RISK (the main thing to watch). This is the third hooded violet robe + skull face + bone hands in the roster after the LICH and the NECROMANCER, and the necromancer is being converted in the same batch. Differentiators to hold seeds to: (a) bell/cone silhouette with a RAGGED SAW-TOOTH hem, not a straight-hanging robe; (b) EMPTY HANDS — the bonecaller has no staff at all; if a seed comes back with a bone staff, reject it, that is the necromancer bleeding through; (c) the hovering skull is the prop, and it belongs beside/above the open hand, not on top of a staff; (d) no crown, no gem, no glowing chest jewel (that is the lich). Also keep it visibly SMALLER/slighter than the necromancer — it is the lesser summoner of the pair.

ROBED-DEATH TRAP: bone is named four times on purpose (skull face, skeletal arm, bone fingers, floating skull) per the lich/necromancer lesson — a robed undead whose bones are not named grows human flesh on the rear-facing death frames. Do not trim those repetitions when tightening the line.

Scale: rank-and-file, no battleScale on the def, so no boss-scale/padding concern. It is an epic-rarity DUNGEON MONSTER (Bonefields floor 1-5, weight 5 — the most common thing in that dungeon), never a deckable card, so it needs the full monster clip set. Its Raise Dead is a passive kit onTick with no cast animation, which is another reason the idle must be a settled rest pose rather than a summon.

units.notes:
SOURCE READ: drawAbomination at src/assets/sprites.ts:6199-6325, dispatched from case "abomination" at :592 with an extra ctx.scale(1.25). Tints come from UnitDef color #6f7a58 ("grey-green stitched flesh") + accent #b7c48a (pale yellow-green), src/data/units.ts:861-888; battleScale 2.1 (boss). Bonefields boss, tags ["undead"].

WHERE SOURCE BEATS THE SUMMARY:
- The summary says "ONE glowing pale eye". The source draws BOTH: a glowing accent-coloured eye (arc r1.5 at -1,-13, shadowBlur pulsed) AND a tiny dead near-black square eye (#141210 at 2.6,-13.6). I wrote it as mismatched eyes — one glowing sickly yellow-green, one small and dark. If a seed renders it as a cyclops that is still on-theme; judge by eye, but reject seeds that give it two symmetric normal eyes (kills the identity).
- The summary omits the MOUTH SEWN SHUT (a horizontal seam with four cross-stitches) — kept, it is the most readable face feature at 45px after the eye.
- Feet are near-black (#26281f), broad and flat, wider than the legs; stitch seams appear at both knees, across the belly and diagonally across the chest.
- The "rusty" shoulder spike is stroked in grey #9a9488, not orange rust — I wrote "rusty grey" to match the pixels, not the code comment.

MID-GESTURE TRANSLATION: none needed, and that is lucky for a boss. The big arm is a fat lineWidth-7 stroke from the shoulder (11,-3) curving out and down to a r5 fist at (16,17) — feet sit at y19-23.5, so the fist genuinely hangs at ground level in the source. "hanging to the ground and ending in a huge fist" is a faithful REST reading, not an invention. Keep it that way; the overhead swing belongs in the attack clip.

ASYMMETRY / MIRROR HAZARD (the important one): this unit is the most asymmetric body in the roster — big arm and shoulder spike on one side, ribs and the withered arm on the other. I deliberately did NOT name left/right so mirrored exports (the archer FACING_SWAP precedent) do not contradict the desc. But it means seed-to-seed the big arm can land on either side, and any nw/ne mirror flips it. Pick ONE side at the idle-promotion step and enforce it across all clips by eye; and remember [[raw-mirror-is-the-anim-seed]] — park rejects in BOTH pixel_raw/dirs mirrors or the idle comes out flipped. Also: promote with --medoid, this is exactly the asymmetric case that rule was written for.

DEATH-CLIP RISK: hulking + wide + a ground-reaching arm means a high seed-still FILL. If FILL >= 0.85 on the promoted still, pad before generating deaths or the rear falls clip. Undead rear-facing deaths are the lich trap: the material is named up front ("rotting grey-green flesh", "bone ribs") on purpose — re-state rotting flesh and bare ribs in the death prompt or WAN grows clean human skin on the fall.

BOSS SCALE: rendered 2.1x in battle on top of the draw-time 1.25x, so generate in the standard 80px cell like everyone else and let the game scale it; do NOT draw it larger in-frame or the wide-margin framing breaks. Expect it to read chunkier than any converted unit — nearest already-converted neighbour is the OGRE (hulking hunched biped); reuse the ogre's prompt scaffolding/rig for proportions, but it is NOT a recolour: different arms, ribs, sunken head, stitches.

DROPPED ON PURPOSE: the rising rot-miasma particles and the two orbiting flies are A.live animation only (rising(...) + the A.live fly loop) — not baked into the sprite. If the user wants the reek visible, do it in the themed-aura pass (like the necromancer aura), not in the DESC, where it fights "exactly one creature, plain pure white background".

units.master_override: A hi-bit pixel art game sprite of {desc}, standing on all four legs with all four paws planted flat on the ground, facing the viewer, its full body and all four legs visible. Seen from a high three-quarter overhead camera tilted about 60 degrees down toward the ground — the classic camera of a top-down 16-bit action role-playing game — so the top of the bristling spine ridge and the top of the head read from above while the face and chest still read clearly. Flat even overhead light, identical on the left and right, no cast shadow. Exactly one creature, centred, drawn small with wide empty margin on all sides, on a plain pure white background.
units.notes:
SOURCE: `drawDireWolf` at src/assets/sprites.ts:6533 (dispatch `case "dire_wolf"` at :597, no ctx.scale — renders at 1.0). UnitDef at src/data/units.ts:930 — color #5b6470 ("slate-grey pelt"), accent #c7ccd4. The draw derives dark = shade(color,-45) ≈ charcoal-slate #2e3743 (tail, legs, paws, hackles, ears) and light = shade(color,+40) ≈ #838c98 (head, top of the body gradient); accent is used ONLY as a 0.5px pale glint on the eye, so I demoted it to "a pale silver glint" rather than a palette pillar. Hard-coded colours I carried over: nose #26282c (near-black), eye #2a2320 (dark brown), fang #f3f3e0 (bone-white).

MID-GESTURE TRANSLATIONS (2): (1) The panting tongue (#c56b7a, drawn only when `A.live`, bobbing on sin(t*4)) is DROPPED from the DESC — it is an idle-animation beat, not identity, and "panting/lolling tongue" reads as a mid-gesture that would poison the attack clip. If you want it, put it in the IDLE clip prompt only. (2) The bared fang is a single static triangle in the source, so I kept it as a feature ("one bone-white fang at the side of its muzzle") and deliberately avoided "snarling"/"bared teeth", which are banned mid-gesture and reliably render an open-mouthed roar into every still.

ALSO DROPPED: the cold-breath `rising(...)` wisps (#dfe6ee, from the muzzle at x=22). Pure liveliness VFX. Good IDLE-clip flavour; keep it out of the identity or WAN will grow a permanent smoke plume that flickers between frames.

BODY REUSE / COLLISION WARNINGS: this is the third wolf-shaped body in the game. (a) The arena `wolf` (Spirit Wolf, drawWolf at :4312) is the SAME species silhouette but a genuinely different pose — crouched pounce, chest dipped, BUSHY RAISED tail, spirit-glow eyes. The dire wolf is an upright prowl with a LOW SWEEPING tail and mundane dark eyes. If the Spirit Wolf has already been converted in the pending six-unit batch, do NOT reuse its frames here: the tail carriage and the eye glow are the two things that separate them at 45px, and both would be wrong. (b) `dire_alpha` (drawDireAlpha at :6847) is the Wilds boss and is explicitly this same body scaled 1.2× with a near-black pelt, blood-red glowing eyes and a red rage aura — when you get to it, this dire wolf's clips are the right starting point, at boss scale.

DEATH-CLIP RISK: the silhouette is unusually WIDE and LOW (body ellipse rx=15 plus a snout out to x=23 and a tail back to x=-21 — roughly 44 units wide against ~28 tall). A quadruped that already fills the cell horizontally is the classic seed-still FILL ≥0.85 case; check FILL on the promoted seed still and pad the death clip before generating, or the rear-fall will clip at the frame edge. Same reason the rear (ne/nw) walk cycles need watching — the tail sweeps outside the body on the diagonals.

MASTER: quadruped ⇒ override required (modelled on the shipped summoner_bear master; only the body clause and the "reads from above" clause differ — camera, light and framing sentences are byte-identical to the shared master). Without it the shared "both legs and both feet planted" clause will stand this wolf up on two legs like a werewolf. "the top of the bristling spine ridge" is the deliberate quadruped analogue of the bear's "shoulder hump" — the hackle ridge IS the top-down read for this unit.

No held props, no headgear ⇒ empty GEAR, no screen-side pin.

units.notes:
SOURCE: src/assets/sprites.ts drawRazorback (L6645-6732), dispatch case at L600; UnitDef at src/data/units.ts L945-959 (color #5a3f2c "bristly brown hide", accent #d9c2a3 "tusks"). Derived tints via withShade: dark = rgb(45,18,0) near-black brown (belly, legs, ear, brow), light = rgb(130,103,84) mid warm brown (body top, head). Hooves/nostrils #2b1c10, eye #1a1a1a, tusks #e8dabc cream ivory. Inventory summary matched the source on all counts (7 spines, 2 tusks, blunt snout, 4 stubby hooved legs) — no disagreement to resolve.

MID-GESTURE TRANSLATIONS I MADE: (1) the procedural art is a strict SIDE PROFILE facing right — one visible ear, one visible eye, tusks read as a big outer + small inner pair. For the viewer-facing master I pluralised to "ears"/"eyes" and "two upward-curving tusks"; the generator would otherwise give a one-eyed boar. (2) The source ends with a `rising()` particle plume from the snout (snorting breath, #d8c3a8) and the header comment calls it "a bristly CHARGING boar ... snorting". Both are animation/liveliness, not identity — I dropped the breath entirely and used no action verb. The snort belongs in the ATTACK/idle clip, not the DESC; do NOT add "snorting" or "charging" back into the prompt or every still comes out mid-lunge.
(3) The "angry brow" stroke survived as a static "heavy dark brow" — deliberately not "snarling"/"scowling".

SHAPE DETAIL WORTH GATING ON: the spine crest is a row of SEVEN triangles with the 4th one +2 taller (a peak in the middle) — hence "tallest at the middle". Exact spine count almost never renders; accept 5-8 evenly-sized triangles if the crest reads as bone plates rather than fins or a shaggy mane. The body is genuinely LOW and WIDE (ellipse 15x8 with legs only 7 units long) — reject seeds that give it long deer legs or a bear-like upright chest.

BODY REUSE / MASTER: quadruped master is imitated from the shipped summoner_bear override, changed only in the body clause ("four hooves") and the reads-from-above clause (spine ridge + head). dire_wolf and grizzly (same Wilds tier) will want the same override text — write it once and share. This is NOT a recolour of an already-converted unit; nothing quadruped has been through the pipeline yet, so budget extra seed rolls (orientation first) — quadrupeds are the untested body plan here and WAN loves to rear them up onto two legs. If a roll comes back bipedal, re-roll rather than editing.

NO BOSS SCALE: razorback has no battleScale (the tier's boss dire_alpha is 2.1, and the dispatch scales it 1.2 in-canvas) — razorback draws at 1.0, so standard cell/pad settings apply. Standard hazards still apply: check the seed's still FILL before the death clip (>=0.85 means pad it), and this silhouette is WIDE — the tusks and the spine crest are the first things to clip on a rear fall.

SOURCE READ: dispatch at src/assets/sprites.ts:607 -> drawGrizzly at :6736-6843. UnitDef at src/data/units.ts:960 (color #7a5a34 "brown bear", accent #e8d3ad, rare, melee "Woodland Bruiser", NO battleScale so no boss-scale risk). In the procedural draw, body=#7a5a34, dark=withShade(-45), light=withShade(+40) — so the shoulder hump, head and chest patch are a LIGHTER BROWN/golden tan, not cream. The literal accent #e8d3ad is used ONLY for the claw strokes (fore paws). The inventory summary's "pale tan #e8d3ad belly and muzzle" is slightly off: the muzzle is withShade(body,+18) (a light tan) and the chest is `light` at 0.25 alpha. I split the difference in the desc — golden-tan hump, pale cream chest/claws, lighter tan muzzle.

MID-GESTURE TRANSLATION PERFORMED: the procedural art is a FRONTAL GROWL (head centered on top of the chest, dark #2a0f0f open mouth, two white triangles, rising "heavy breath" puff). Per the summary's ** warning I converted it to an ordinary four-legged bear at rest via the summoner_bear master. The growl survives ONLY as a static facial read ("two short white triangular fangs in a dark open mouth") — same register as the shipped summoner_bear desc, which uses "an open growling mouth showing white fangs" and generated fine. The roar/rear-up belongs in the ATTACK clip. Breath puffs are engine liveliness, deliberately not in the desc.

REUSE / COLLISION RISK — READ THIS BEFORE GENERATING: this body is very close to `summoner_bear` (the Druid's Bear Form, drawBear at sprites.ts:4533), which is in the same batch queue. If both are rolled the pair can come back looking like the same sprite in two tints. The deliberate divergences, all backed by the source: summoner_bear is HUGE and SHAGGY (an explicit spiked-fur silhouette pass), dark, with GLOWING PALE-GREEN eyes and a green nature-magic backlight; the grizzly is a smaller, SMOOTH-coated mundane bear (pure ellipses, no fur spikes) with small dark natural eyes, a glossy black nose and pale cream claws, and no aura of any kind. Keep "dense"/"smooth" out of the bear-form prompt and "shaggy" out of this one. If seeds keep converging, the cheapest fix is to reuse the already-converted summoner_bear geometry and re-roll only for the tint/eyes rather than burning fresh seeds — but do NOT palette-swap the shipped bear-form strips into `grizzly`, the hump/head scale differs.

PIPELINE TODO: add "grizzly" to UNITS in tools/pixel_fa.py AND an entry to MASTER_BY_UNIT (the shared master's "both legs and both feet planted" clause rears a bear upright — this is the exact failure the wolf and summoner_bear overrides exist for). Quadruped, so it needs the diagonal-4 se+ne runbook, and watch the known "the-generic-walk-walks-at-the-camera" trap on the walk clip — a bear's four-beat gait is the hardest thing here. It is the first WILDS-tier unit to be converted; dire_wolf, razorback and the dire_alpha boss (2.1x battleScale) are still procedural, so the tier will look mixed in game until they follow.

units.notes:
SOURCE: src/assets/sprites.ts drawDireAlpha (line 6847), dispatched at line 603 with ctx.scale(1.2). UnitDef at src/data/units.ts:976 — color #3f4550 (body), accent #ef4444; the drawer derives dark = withShade(body,-45) ≈ #121823 (near-black blue-black, used for tail/legs/hackles/ears) and light = withShade(body,+40) ≈ #676d78 (slate grey, used for the head and the top of the body gradient). "near-black slate-grey fur" in the DESC covers that whole ramp. Confirms the inventory summary in every particular.

NO MID-GESTURE TRANSLATION WAS NEEDED — unusually for a boss, the base pose is a static standing side profile. Every feature I kept is a pose-free identity trait: the tail is drawn already raised (a dominance carry, moveTo(-13,4) → quad-curve to (-20,-11)), the hackles are already up, and the fangs are a fixed pair of cream triangles under the upper jaw with a dark mouth line, not an animated snarl. I wrote "two cream-white fangs over its lip" rather than "bared fangs mid-snarl" so nothing reads as a roar; the roar belongs in the attack clip.

TWO EFFECTS DELIBERATELY OMITTED from the DESC, both intentional:
1. The blood-red radial "rage backlight" (globalAlpha 0.16 + 0.06*glow, radius 26). Naming a red glow/haze around the body risks tinting the plain-pure-white background and blowing the background gate. Ship this the way the necromancer aura shipped — a game-side themed aura in the renderer/export, not a prompt token. It is on the open "themed legendary auras" to-do anyway.
2. The rising red panting-heat particles at the muzzle (rising(ctx,24,3,3,10,accent,A,2)) — pure liveliness VFX, presentation-only, never a sprite feature.

BODY REUSE: this is structurally a heavier drawDireWolf (line 6533) — same construction order (tail → four bent-hock legs → paw ellipses → body ellipse over the leg tops → hackles → skull circle + triangular snout → two ears). dire_wolf is NOT yet converted. If it comes later, do NOT recolour or reuse the alpha's frames: the alpha differs by a RAISED tail (the wolf's sweeps LOW, to (-20,13)), taller and more numerous hackles (3 peaks spanning x 0→16 vs 2 peaks x 3→13), a glowing red eye vs a mundane eye with a white shine, bared fangs, a shoulder scar, and a visibly broader barrel (16x8 vs 15x6.5). The wolf should be rangier and plainer.

BOSS SCALE: battleScale 2.1 in units.ts plus the 1.2 sprite scale — both are game-side, so generate at the normal 80px cell. But the silhouette is wide (long body + raised tail + tall hackles ≈ full cell width). Per the seed-still-FILL rule, check the still's fill ratio before promoting; at ≥0.85 pad the death clips or the fall will clip the frame edge. Quadruped rear-facing deaths are exactly where an unnamed material grows human flesh, which is why "fur" is named at the front of the DESC and the pelt is re-described — do not trim either mention in a later edit.

ONE EYE vs TWO: the procedural art is a side profile and draws a single red eye. The front-facing master will produce two glowing red eyes, which is correct and matches what the inventory pass decided mattered.

WORD RISK: "hackles" is a low-frequency token. I hedged it with "a row of tall jagged raised hackles bristling along its spine" so the shape still renders if the word itself carries little weight. If seeds come back with a smooth spine, swap to "a row of tall jagged spikes of stiff raised fur along its spine" and re-roll rather than adding a new concept.

Walk/attack suggestion: this is the second quadruped after summoner_bear — expect the same generic-walk-walks-at-the-camera trap on the front-facing directions. Judge steadiness on the body mass, not the tail (a heavy raised tail will read as drift). Wrap-jump ≤0.9x step size as usual. Attack = the lunging bite the procedural art implies but never draws.

units.notes:
SOURCE READ IN FULL: sprites.ts dispatch line 610 (`case "apex_beast"` → `ctx.scale(1.15)` → `drawApexBeast`, lines 6979-7120). UnitDef in src/data/units.ts lines 1002-1016: legendary, role "Great Predator", `color: "#6b4a2a"` (great brown bear), `accent: "#f5e0b8"` (pale cream), NO battleScale. It is the rare Wilds floor-5 quest catalyst (WILDS_QUEST in data/dungeons.ts) and also sits in ENDLESS_RARE_POOL, so it spawns as a miniboss event.

MID-GESTURE TRANSLATIONS I PERFORMED (three of them, all deliberate):
1. POSTURE. The procedural art is REARED UP FRONTALLY on two hind feet — haunches ellipse at y=10, hind feet at y=20, forelegs hanging down the SIDES at x=±13, chest ellipse at y=-2, head centred at y=-13. Per the inventory warning I translated it to a standing quadruped (the shipped `summoner_bear` precedent). If you ever want the reared silhouette back it must be the ATTACK clip, never the DESC.
2. ROARING MAW. Source draws an open dark-red maw (#2a0f0f) with four white fangs (two upper triangles at x=±2.6, two lower at x=±1.6). "Snarling mid-roar" is banned, so the fangs survive as "long white fangs" on a closed pale-cream muzzle. Note `summoner_bear` shipped with "an open growling mouth showing white fangs" — if the closed muzzle reads too tame at 45px you may borrow that phrasing, but expect it to bleed into the idle.
3. AURA + EMBERS OMITTED. The source wraps it in a primal amber radial aura (alpha 0.18 + 0.07·glow, r=30) and `rising(...)` amber embers — a liveliness/catalyst signature, not silhouette. Baking a glow halo into the DESC fouls the white-background alpha cutout and the pixelize palette lock. This unit is exactly the case the handoff's "themed legendary auras" game-side task exists for — add the amber aura in the Renderer, not in the prompt.

WHAT I KEPT AS IDENTITY: the huge shoulder hump (source: light-fill ellipse 9.5×5.5 at y=-8, the biggest hump of any bear draw), the blazing amber #ffb43a eyes with a glow blur (the one hardcoded colour that is NOT the UnitDef accent), the great ivory claws (hardcoded "#f0e2c0", lineWidth 2, three per fore paw, 3.5 units long — noticeably longer/thicker than the Grizzly's 2.5 at lineWidth 1.4), and the three claw-scars raked across the chest. Hump-above-head: the raw canvas actually puts the head crown (y≈-22.5) above the hump crown (y≈-13.5), so the inventory summary is wrong on the literal geometry — but on a QUADRUPED the grizzly hump correctly rides above the head line, and the master override's "top of the shoulder hump and the top of the head read from above" depends on it, so I wrote the hump higher on purpose.

REUSE / COLLISION RISK: this body is very close to the already-converted `summoner_bear` (Druid Bear Form) and to the unconverted `grizzly` (drawGrizzly, line 6736 — same construction at ~80% scale). Do NOT let the seeds converge: summoner_bear is dark brown with pale-GREEN eyes and a spiky ragged coat; apex_beast is warmer deep-brown, AMBER-eyed, scarred, and visibly bigger-humped. If you medoid-promote, check the eye colour on the promoted frame before export — an amber/green mixup here is invisible in the strip and glaring in game.

SCALE / FILL: no `battleScale`, but the dispatch applies `ctx.scale(1.15)` — it draws ~15% larger than rank-and-file, and the sprite pipeline's 80px cell is unaffected. Still watch FILL: a wide quadruped with the hump plus four splayed paws is a broad silhouette; per the "seed-still-FILL-predicts-death-clipping" rule, if the still lands ≥0.85 fill, pad BEFORE generating the death clips (a bear collapsing sideways sweeps a lot wider than a biped).

CLIP NOTES: quadruped death = a compact sideways collapse (approved taste anchor), not a human-style backward fall — and the DESC names fur explicitly so the rear-facing clips keep a pelt instead of growing human flesh. The attack clip is where the rear-up + roar + downward claw swipe belongs; that is the one place the source's actual pose should be reproduced. Diagonal-4 only (se/ne, mirrored), per the roster standard.

units.notes:
SOURCE: C:/Users/Justin/Documents/fantasy-arena/src/assets/sprites.ts:7127 drawArcaneWisp (dispatch case at :615); UnitDef at src/data/units.ts:1025 (color #7c5cff violet = `body`, accent #c4b5fd lavender; `dark` is explicitly voided, `light` = withShade(body,+40) = pale violet). Stroke inventory, in draw order: (1) accent lavender halo disc r=15 at alpha 0.28 centred (0,-4); (2) four zig-zag lightning tendrils in pale violet, lineWidth 1, from r=3 out to r=12, 90 degrees apart — GATED ON A.live, so a still frame has NO tendrils; I kept them in the DESC anyway because the inventory pass flagged them as identity and the sprite reads wrong without them; (3) the 8-point star core, alternating r=8/r=4, filled violet with a lavender glow; (4) a near-white #f0ecff glint disc r=2.4 offset up-left at (-1.5,-5.5); (5) a lavender rune-shard dot r=1.6 orbiting on a 12x7 ellipse. Everything is centred at y=-4, i.e. it already sits above the ground origin — it hovers. There is no head, face, torso or limb geometry anywhere in the function.

MID-GESTURE TRANSLATION: none needed. All motion in the source is ambient (core spin t*0.5, tendril jitter, shard orbit t*2.5), not a gesture, so the DESC is already a rest identity. No banned verbs used; "radiating" replaced an earlier "reaching" (R1 banned word).

NEW BODY PLAN — HIGHEST RISK ITEM. There is no shipped floating-wisp master; I wrote the override from scratch on the slime pattern (camera/light/framing sentences verbatim, only the body clause and the "reads from above" clause changed). The whole defence against WAN growing legs/feet/a face is the positive wording "bodiless mote", "floating clear of the ground with open empty air beneath it", and "Exactly one glowing mote". At cfg=1 a bad roll must be answered with a NEW SEED, not with an added negation. Expect the rear-facing death clips to be where a face/body tries to appear (the usual failure) — the material is named twice on purpose.

ORIENTATION JUDGING BREAKS HERE. The mote is radially symmetric, so se/ne/sw/nw stills will be near-identical and the usual "orientation first" review criterion barely applies. The only asymmetries in the source are the up-left glint and the orbiting shard, and neither is directional. Judge picks on core crispness, spike count (8 — count them, generators love 5- and 6-point stars) and halo evenness instead. FACING_SWAP is almost certainly a no-op for this unit.

WALK CLIPS. A mote has no stride, so the BEAM_CYCLE_WALK exporter cut (judge loops by wrap-jump / step-size) is meaningless here — do not fail a clip for a missing leg cycle, and the lich's "he should be walking" rejection explicitly does NOT transfer. Correct motion is a level glide with a small vertical bob plus tendril flicker; if the roll makes it lurch or sprout feet, re-roll.

DEATH CLIP. corpseArt.ts:114 maps arcane_wisp -> "dissipate", so the death should scatter/fade outward rather than fall over. FILL WARNING: the halo is r=15 against a core of r=8, so the still already fills a lot of the cell before a dissipate burst expands — check seed FILL and pad the death (the >=0.85 clipping rule applies).

SOURCE: sprites.ts:7757-7833 (drawThornbeast), dispatched at :648; UnitDef units.ts:1151 (color #3f6b2f mossy hide, accent #8fae52 thorns, rare, MELEE, moveSpeed 65, ability "lifesteal" = passive filler, it never casts). NO MID-GESTURE TRANSLATION NEEDED. Unlike the wolf (crouched, chest dipped, mid-lunge), the thornbeast's procedural body is a plain static ellipse with the head forward — it is already at rest. The charge/gore belongs in the ATTACK clip; the DESC deliberately avoids "charger" even though its in-game role string is "Bramble Charger".

⚠ SYMMETRY CALL — the one place I departed from the source. The procedural art draws exactly ONE eye and ONE thorn tusk, because it is a right-facing side profile; I wrote both as PAIRS for the front-facing master. Reason: the shared LIGHT clause exists so the sprite survives a horizontal mirror, and export_to_game.py's FACING_SWAP actually ships mirrored facings — a single eye or single tusk jumps from one side of the face to the other between mirrored directions, which reads as an identity flip at 45px. If the human wants a cyclops beast instead (note the shipped legacy wolf DESC kept "a glowing green eye" singular), edit to "a single glowing yellow-green eye" and "a short olive thorn tusk hooking upward from one side of the snout", and expect the mirror problem.

⚠ PADDING: the six spine thorns and the two leaves stick up ABOVE the body silhouette and are the whole read at 45px — they are the first thing a tight crop eats. Use --pad on every clip and run the seed-still FILL check before the death roll (≥0.85 ⇒ pad more); a compact quadruped death that tips sideways will swing the thorn ridge wide.

DELIBERATE OMISSION: the drifting pale-lime spore motes (rising(ctx, 0, 12, 10, 22, "#d9f99d", A, 3)) are runtime liveliness, not silhouette. Left out so the generator does not scatter particles over the pure-white background. If the user wants them they belong in a themed aura pass (like the necromancer's), not in the DESC.

NOT a recolour of any converted unit. Nearest converted neighbour by body plan is summoner_bear — I copied its quadruped master construction verbatim and changed only the "reads from above" landmark (shoulder hump -> thorn ridge). Do not reuse the bear's clips: it is a tall shaggy fur mammal, this is a low, long, legs-barely-visible plant hog, so its walk cycle is a short-stride trot with almost no vertical bob.

units.notes:
SOURCE: drawSporePod at C:/Users/Justin/Documents/fantasy-arena/src/assets/sprites.ts:1447 (dispatch case line 651, no ctx.scale — normal scale, zero boss-scale risk). Palette rendered as adjectives accordingly.

NO MID-GESTURE TRANSLATION NEEDED — the procedural art is already a rest pose. Its only motion is the idle: the cap ellipse breathes +/-5% (A.t*2) and the five spore spots pulse in alpha. That IS the idle clip brief: a slow swell/deflate of the cap with the lime spots pulsing. Nothing to re-pose.

TWO DETAILS THE INVENTORY SUMMARY MISSED, both in the desc: (1) seven faint darker gill lines splaying under the cap rim (alpha 0.4 — low priority, will likely vanish at 45px, do not fail a gate on them); (2) a pale highlight ellipse on the UPPER-LEFT of the cap. The highlight being left-biased means mirrored facings flip it — harmless, ignore.

DELIBERATELY OMITTED: the procedural sprite emits a rising pale-lime spore cloud off the cap (rising() at line 1500). Left out of the DESC on R1 grounds — "puffing" is a gesture and a floating cloud wrecks the small-silhouette read and the white-background alpha cut. Put the puff in the ATTACK clip, and expect the game to want a particle to replace it once the pixel sprite lands.

NO FACE — handled positively ("the whole cap a smooth unbroken shell"); the word "face" never appears, and neither does "eyes". Watch the rear/death frames: this is exactly where an unnamed material grows human flesh, hence "spongy fungal flesh" is stated up front. If a gate frame sprouts eyes or a mouth on the cap, re-roll the seed rather than editing.

⚠ WALK CYCLE — THE SUMMARY IS WRONG ON THIS. It claims "no walk cycle will ever be generated (turret precedent)", but units.ts gives spore_pod moveSpeed 24 ("barely creeps") and range: MELEE, so the engine DOES walk it across the field toward a target. It is not a turret. Decide before the batch: either generate a minimal creep (roots shuffling, cap bobbing — do NOT let the generator give it legs; the rooted master must stay in force) or map walk -> idle in export_to_game.py and let it slide at 24px/s. My recommendation is the latter; a 24-speed mushroom sliding is readable and a legged mushroom is not.

FACINGS: a radially symmetric dome has no true front, so the four diagonals will come out near-identical. Roll one good se and mirror rather than burning seeds on ne/nw. Judge steadiness on the CAP CENTRE, not the silhouette — the breathing ellipse will trip a silhouette-based steadiness judge every time.

SILHOUETTE RISK: the cap is 32px wide against a 38px total height (cap 32x26, stalk 8x18, roots 3px). It is genuinely squat and wide — wider than it is tall above the stalk. If seeds come back as tall slender storybook mushrooms, that is the failure mode; "squat wide-capped" is doing that work in the desc and can be strengthened to "toadstool that is wider than it is tall" on a re-roll.

FIRST ROOTED-PLANT BODY IN THE ROSTER — no recolour, no reuse. The master override above is new; it is written from the turret precedent with the machine nouns swapped for plant nouns. The Overgrowth tier's other plants (elder_treant, dryad) are humanoid-ish trees and should NOT inherit this master unmodified. DEATH CLIP: budget padding — a rooted body that collapses sideways sweeps well outside the idle bounding box; if the seed-still FILL is >=0.85, pad before generating the death.

units.notes:
SOURCE: drawDryad at src/assets/sprites.ts:7837 (dispatch case at :654); UnitDef at src/data/units.ts:1185 — color #2f6b4a (deep leaf-green), accent #bbf7d0 (mint). Renderer derives dark = shade(-45) and light = shade(+40), so the trunk is a top-lit gradient: mossy mid-green at the shoulders darkening to near-black forest green at the base; root prongs are drawn in the darkest green; the head is filled with the LIGHT green with its lower half slightly darkened.

MID-GESTURE TRANSLATION I PERFORMED: in the procedural art BOTH branch-arms are lifted up and outward (left arm curves to -10,-9; right to 11,-8) and the heal-bloom sits at (-11,-10), i.e. in a RAISED hand at head height — that is the Mend cast, not a rest pose. I translated it to arms angled out as low boughs with the bloom cradled against the chest. The raised-bloom-overhead pose belongs in the ATTACK/cast clip; do not bake it into the master still.

NO LEGS IN THE SOURCE — this is why the override exists. The torso is ONE solid tapering slab from y=-8 to y=20 (no leg split anywhere), ending in three short prongs: one splaying left, one straight down, one splaying right. The shared master's "both legs and both feet planted" will grow two human legs and delete the trunk. Keep the three roots — the inventory pass capitalised THREE for a reason.

THE BODY-PLAN LABEL LIES ABOUT MOBILITY. I picked "rooted-static" only because that is the enum bucket that forces the override; the dryad is NOT static. It is a mobile ranged healer (moveSpeed 55, range 0.3x field width, Cleric "mend" kit) and needs the full diagonal walk set. Walk = the three root prongs alternately lifting and stumping down with the trunk swaying slightly. Do NOT accept a hover or a glide: the lich burned five iterations on exactly this and the user's verdict was "he should be walking". Judge the wrap-jump at <=0.9x step size.

MATERIAL DISCIPLINE: the head is a small green WOODEN knob (light-green fill, darker lower half), not skin. If a seed rolls a pale human face or bare flesh limbs, re-roll — unnamed/underweighted material is what grows flesh on the rear-facing death clips.

BODY REUSE: none. This is a fresh body, not a recolour of any converted unit. Its Overgrowth tier-mates elder_treant (drawn at ctx.scale 1.4) and wildheart (1.1) are separate, much bulkier draws — do not reuse this slender trunk for them.

SCALE: normal. The dispatch applies no scale multiplier to dryad, so no boss-scale framing risk.

DEATH CLIP: run the seed-still FILL check and pad generously. The silhouette is tall and narrow (trunk + leaf crown, ~31px of drawn height above the roots); a rear fall lays that whole column out and will clip at >=0.85 fill.

SPAWN CLIP MATTERS MORE THAN USUAL: the Wildheart's Final Bloom buds TWO dryads on death (verdant_pulse), so this sprite pops into an active fight as a summon — a clean spawn/idle handoff is worth a take.

GEAR SIDE: the bloom is on SCREEN-LEFT in the procedural art (the character's own right hand when facing the viewer). I recorded hand "left" as a screen-side pin only; the side is arbitrary for a non-aimed prop, so if FACING_SWAP mirrors the ne/nw exports the flower simply changes sides — harmless here, unlike the archer's bow.

LIVELINESS the master cannot carry: the game draws rising mint heal-motes and a pulsing glow on the bloom and eyes. Those are engine-side particles (rising(), A.glow) — leave them to the game, do not try to prompt drifting motes into the sprite frames.
