units.notes:
SOURCE READ IN FULL: dispatch at src/assets/sprites.ts:657 (ctx.scale 1.4) -> drawElderTreant at src/assets/sprites.ts:1311-1443. UnitDef at src/data/units.ts:1208 — color "#5b4327" (bark brown, = body; dark = shade -45, light = shade +40) and accent "#4d7c0f" (canopy green; canopyD = -28, canopyL = +30, plus a bright #fde68a eye glow). The inventory summary is accurate; source adds bark-grain lines, a translucent moss patch low on the trunk, a knotted/lumpy trunk rim, and three clawed twigs per hand.

MID-GESTURE TRANSLATION I PERFORMED: the procedural arms sweep from mid-trunk (±6,-3) out and UP to (±19,-17) with splayed claws — in the game art that reads as a raised threat pose. I translated it to a static bough spread ("angled up and out"), which is a tree's natural rest silhouette and keeps the wide, recognisable branch-arm shape. The actual grab/slam belongs in the ATTACK clip; note the ability is Grasping Roots (roots erupt from the ground), so an arms-down / root-surge attack reads better than an overhead swing.

WHY THE MASTER OVERRIDE (minimal, two clauses only): this unit HAS two legs and two arms, so the body clause is kept nearly verbatim ("both root legs and both root feet planted"). The only real change is the reads-from-above clause: the shared master's "the tops of the shoulders and the top of the head ... while the face and chest read clearly" will grow a HEAD above the canopy and float the face up into it. The face is carved LOW on the trunk (brow at y≈-3.5, eyes y≈+1, mouth y≈+8, while the trunk spans -12..+20) — roughly chest height, with the canopy sitting directly on the trunk where a head would be. Camera/light/framing sentences are byte-identical to the shipped masters.

IDENTITY RISK #1 — face height. Every still must be checked for the face drifting up into the canopy. If it does, the fix is in the master clause, not the DESC.
IDENTITY RISK #2 — rear-facing clips will show plain bark with NO face. That is CORRECT for this unit; do not "fix" it. Do check that the rear falls stay wood — an unnamed material is what grows flesh, and "living wood"/"bark"/"trunk" are named three times for exactly that reason.

BOSS SCALE / FILL: battleScale 2.1 in-game and ctx.scale 1.4 at the sprite call site — this is the biggest Overgrowth body. Combined with the wide canopy and the outstretched branch arms, the silhouette is WIDE, not tall. Expect high FILL on the seed; per the ≥0.85 rule, PAD THE DEATH CLIP, and eyeball the walk/attack strips for branch-tip crop at the left/right edges.

DEATH CLIP: corpseArt.ts:97 maps elder_treant -> "stump". The death should topple/collapse toward a broken stump, not a humanoid sprawl. Worth saying so in the death prompt.

NOT A RECOLOUR: no already-converted body fits this. Do NOT reuse this body for `wildheart` (the other treant-family unit, units.ts:1239, color #6b5327 / accent #facc15 golden sap) — it has its own draw function (drawWildheart) and a different silhouette; read it separately.

ANIMATION-ONLY DETAILS TO KEEP OUT OF THE SPRITE: the drifting leaves (A.live block) and the pulsing eye glow are engine liveliness VFX. Do not bake falling leaves into the idle frames — they will flicker and desync across clips. Keep the eyes lit but steady in the stills.

MID-GESTURE TRANSLATION: minor. The procedural art is already a rest pose (no swing, no lunge), but the branch-arms are drawn raised outward, which a generator can read as a gesture. I pinned them with positional phrasing — "angled out from its shoulders" — instead of any verb of motion. Do NOT rewrite that to "raised", "reaching" or "spread wide"; the raised-arm pose belongs in the attack clip. There is no held prop of any kind, so GEAR is deliberately empty: the leaf crown, the twig-claws and the heart-core are all grown body parts, not gear, and pinning them screen-side would be wrong.

NOT A RECOLOUR. drawWildheart is its own function and shares no geometry with the Overgrowth boss drawElderTreant (line 1311), which has TWO root-claw legs, a canopy, and ctx.scale 1.4. Do not reuse a converted treant body for this one — wildheart is short and squat where the treant is colossal. Also note wildheart has NO battleScale field (elder_treant has 2.1), so there is no boss-scale risk here despite its legendary rarity; it fights at rank-and-file size at ctx.scale 1.1.

RARITY GOTCHA: the code comments call it "the rare Overgrowth catalyst" — that means rare *spawn* (the Cleric + Wildheart fusion quest that yields the Druid). The UnitDef rarity field is legendary, so it is in scope for the themed legendary-aura pass.

FILL / DEATH-CLIP WARNING: the sprite draws a golden radial aura of radius 28 centred at (0,-2) — wider than the body itself — plus a glow-blurred heart, glow-blurred eyes, and rising gold pollen. The DESC's "haloed in a soft golden glow" will inflate apparent coverage against the white background. Watch the seed-still FILL prediction; if it lands >=0.85, pad the death clip before generating, or the fall clips the frame. If the themed legendary-aura pass is going to own the halo separately, consider dropping the final four words rather than double-glowing it.

FACE / PROPORTION WATCH: the head is a 5px circle sitting directly on the trunk with no neck, lower half shaded, with two short angled dark brow strokes (the "fierce" read) and two 1.1px glowing pale-yellow eyes. That is a very low head-to-body ratio. If a seed hands back a human-scale head or a human face on a wooden body, re-roll — the material words ("living heartwood", "bark face") are the flesh guard and they are what keeps the rear-facing death clip from growing skin, per the lich/necromancer lesson.

WALK CLIPS: three roots give no clean two-beat cycle; expect the generator to cycle the two outer roots and drag the centre one. Judge steadiness on the trunk body, not the root silhouette. Walk is real (moveSpeed 40) — do not treat this as a rooted/static plant despite the roots.

BODY_PLAN FIELD: I chose "multi-legged" because the ground contact is three-point, which is what the field drives. It is NOT a spider sprawl — it is an upright torso on three roots, so use the master_override written above rather than any stock spider master.

IN-GAME ANIMATION IS NOT SPRITE ANIMATION: the heart-core pulses, the sap-cracks fade in and out, and gold pollen rises — all driven by SpriteAnim at runtime. The DESC describes them as static bright gold on purpose; do not ask for pulsing or motion in the still.

units.notes:
SOURCE vs SUMMARY: they agree. Confirmed in drawLightWisp (sprites.ts:8063-8118): 0.3-alpha gold halo disc r=14 at y=-4; 8 rays stroked in `light` from r=6 to r=11 (even i) / r=13 (odd i) — i.e. alternating long/short, which the summary missed and I put in the DESC; gold core r=5.5 with a pulsing bloom; a near-white highlight blob r=2.4 offset up-left at (-1.4,-5.4); 3 sparks r=0.9 orbiting at r=10. UnitDef (units.ts:1273-1287): color #fcd34d, accent #fffbeb — but `accent` and `dark` are BOTH `void`ed in the draw, so the only two colours on screen are gold #fcd34d and its +40 shade (pale near-white gold). Do NOT let a generation add cream cloth/wings/wisps of smoke in the accent colour; there is no accent geometry.

MID-GESTURE TRANSLATION: none needed for pose — the mote has no gesture. But the art's rays SLOWLY ROTATE (`ctx.rotate(t*0.4)`) and the sparks orbit; per R4 (spin/turn banned) I froze both into a static crown + a suspended ring. The rotation and the twinkle belong in the IDLE clip, and idle is where this unit's whole personality lives — ask for a slow ray drift + spark shimmer there.

NEW BODY PLAN — no precedent. This is the first FLOATING WISP; master override written above (only the body clause and the "reads from above" clause differ from the shared master; camera/light/framing sentences are verbatim). Biggest risk by far: a glowing orb with an off-centre highlight reads to WAN as an EYEBALL, and a "mote" with no named anatomy invites a cartoon face, little arms, or a tinkerbell fairy body. HARD-KILL any seed that shows eyes, a mouth, a fairy silhouette, wings, or legs — this trap is the light-material analogue of the robed-death-grows-flesh bug. Roll best-of-N generously here; the seed still beats the prompt.

ORIENTATION / DIAGONAL-4: the body is radially symmetric, so se/ne/sw/nw will look near-identical and the usual "orientation first" eyeball gate has nothing to grip. Two consequences — (a) FACING_SWAP / left-handed-weapon concerns are moot, (b) whoever runs the batch should judge these clips on motion direction and steadiness, not on which way the body points. Consider generating se + ne only and mirroring, as with the other diagonal-4 units.

EXPORT HAZARDS: pure-light subject on a pure-white background. The halo is only 0.3 alpha and the highlight is near-white — expect the alpha cutout to eat the halo edge and possibly the sparks, and expect very high still-FILL from the bloom (the ≥0.85 FILL rule predicts death-clipping, so PAD the death clip up front). If the matte comes out ragged, judge/promote on the gold core rather than the silhouette (the judge-steadiness-on-the-BODY-not-the-silhouette lesson applies doubly here).

WALK/DEATH TEMPLATES: it has no legs, so BEAM_CYCLE_WALK's wrap-jump ÷ step-size heuristic is meaningless — the walk is a HOVER DRIFT; judge it by lateral travel and a steady hover height, and do not let the generator add a bob so big it reads as a bounce. Death should be a collapse/dim/scatter of the orb (core shrinks, rays retract, sparks fly out) rather than a body falling over — say so in the death prompt or WAN will invent a body to topple.

REUSE: not a recolour of any converted unit. It IS described in-code as "the warm counterpart to the crackling Arcane Wisp" — if arcane_wisp is ever converted, reuse this exact body/master with a violet-blue palette and crackling arcs in place of the smooth halo. Also note units.ts says the Eclipse Spire tier "reuses the wisp draw" with light & dark tints, so this sprite may need a dark-tinted sibling later; check before generating a second one from scratch.

SCALE: rare fodder monster (hp 32), no battleScale entry — no boss-scale risk.

units.notes:
SOURCE READ IN FULL: sprites.ts:8120-8230 (drawShadowWraith), dispatch at :669 with NO ctx.scale — normal rank-and-file size, and units.ts:1288 has no battleScale, so no boss-scale risk. Palette confirmed from the def: color #2a2140 (body, "umbral shade") -> dark = withShade(-45) = near-black violet (hood, wings, horns via -20), light = withShade(+40) (the lighter top of the cloak gradient); accent #7c3aed (aura rim + eye shadowBlur) and a hardcoded eye glow #a970ff. Hence "near-black umbral violet" + "glowing violet eyes".

DISAGREEMENT WITH THE INVENTORY SUMMARY — trust the source: the summary calls the wings "two small ragged bat-wing tatters". They are NOT small. Each wing runs from (±4,-4) out to ±18 with a 4-point jagged trailing edge, while the cloak body only spans ±10. The wings roughly DOUBLE the silhouette width and are the single most recognisable shape at 45px, so the DESC gives them full weight ("wide tattered bat wings ... spread to either side"). Everything else in the summary matched the source (4 hem points, horned peaked hood, face-void + 2 violet eyes, armless, hovering).

MID-GESTURE TRANSLATIONS: none needed — the procedural art is already a rest hover. Two live-only elements were deliberately LEFT OUT of the DESC: (1) the pulsing radial dark aura (r≈23, violet rim) and (2) the four rising dark wisp motes. Both are A.live animation passes, and a hazy bloom in the still would soften the silhouette and fight the pure-white-background alpha cut. Re-add the aura GAME-side as a themed aura (same route as the necromancer aura from round 2), not in the prompt.

R2 COMPLIANCE: "no arms" is never stated — armlessness is carried positively by "falling smooth and unbroken from its shoulders". Same for legs: the master says it floats with a gap of open air beneath the hem.

MASTER OVERRIDE IS UNPRECEDENTED — this is the pipeline's FIRST hovering body plan (MASTER_BY_UNIT currently has only slime/slime_clone/turret/summoner_bear). Camera/light/framing sentences are byte-identical to the shipped masters; only the body clause and the "reads from above" clause differ. Watch the first roll for (a) feet growing under the hem anyway and (b) the model planting it on the ground — if either shows, roll more seeds before editing the text (the-seed-still-beats-the-prompt).

I chose body_plan "hovering-legless" over "floating-wisp": it has a definite cloak body, hood and wings, so it is a shade, not a bodiless mote. The sibling `light_wisp` in the same dungeon tier IS the true floating-wisp case — do not reuse this master for it verbatim (its "top of the hood and its horns" clause is wraith-specific).

CLIP WARNINGS for whoever generates: (1) FILL/clipping — the wings make this one of the widest non-boss silhouettes yet; expect a high seed-still fill ratio, so pad the DEATH clip (>=0.85 => pad) and check the attack clip for wingtip crop. (2) WALK = a GLIDE, not a march. This is the one unit where the lich's "he should be walking" note INVERTS: moveSpeed 92 is fast and it has no legs, so a forward drift with a trailing hem and damped wing flap is correct; do not let a leg cycle appear. (3) DEATH should dissolve/scatter into shadow — corpseArt.ts:118 maps shadow_wraith to the "stain" decal, so a collapse into a dark ground stain matches the game. Naming "solid shadow" and "torn shadow" twice in the DESC is the deliberate guard against WAN growing human flesh or bones on the rear-facing falls (the robed-death trap). (4) The wings will occlude the hood on the ne/nw rear views — judge steadiness on the BODY, not the wing silhouette.

SCOPE NOTE: this is a MONSTER (Eclipse Spire tier, rare spawn — quests.ts:115, dungeons.ts:360), not part of the 22-deckable batch the current handoff mandates. It is not a recolour of any converted unit — no existing body can be reused for it.

units.notes:
SOURCE READ IN FULL: drawEclipseAcolyte at C:/Users/Justin/Documents/fantasy-arena/src/assets/sprites.ts:8234 (dispatch case at :672, no ctx.scale — normal rank-and-file size, no boss-scale risk). MID-GESTURE TRANSLATION I PERFORMED: the procedural art draws both arms as 4px round-cap strokes angled inward and down, (-6,0)->(-1,7) and (6,0)->(1,7), converging on the sigil at (0,8) — the code comment calls it "channelling". I translated that to the rest stance "both hands together low at its chest where a small eclipse sigil floats". The lift/flare belongs in the ATTACK clip: hands rising to the disc, disc brightening. Do NOT put "channelling"/"casting" back into the DESC.

EYE COLOUR — DELIBERATE DEVIATION FROM RENDERED PIXELS, READ THIS. The two eyes are drawn with `light` (left, at -1.4,-8) and `accent` (right, at 1.4,-8). `light` is computed as withShade(body, +40) = rgb(116,69,189), i.e. a brightened VIOLET, not gold — so the shipped pixels are two violet-family eyes and the "mismatch" barely reads. The draw function's own header comment says "one gold eye, one violet", and the inventory pass flagged MISMATCHED as the identity beat. I wrote "one pale gold and one pale lavender" so the mismatch survives at 45px tall. If you want strict rendered-pixel fidelity instead, swap "pale gold" for "bright violet" — but expect the two eyes to read identical and the unit to lose its hook. Same call applies to the sigil: source fills its lit half with `light` (violet); I wrote it neutrally as "a thin crescent of pale light" so it stays eclipse-legible without committing to gold.

CUT FROM THE DESC ON PURPOSE: (a) the sigil's rotating 8-spoke corona (rays from r=5 to r=7.5, animated alpha) — eight radiating spokes at this scale will grow into a sun/wheel/gear and swallow the disc; add it to the ATTACK clip only if you want it. (b) the ambient rising() call at the end — 3 pale-lavender motes drifting up from y=16 — that is presentation-only aura, belongs to a game-side themed aura, not the sprite.

ROBED-CASTER BODY REUSE: this is the third hooded robed caster after the lich and necromancer — every trap from those two applies directly. (1) The robe here is a legless cone (apex at y=-13, widening to +/-10 at y=20) with a 7-point saw-tooth hem and NO feet drawn; the shared master's "both legs and both feet planted" will grow feet under the hem, exactly as it did for the lich/necromancer, and that shipped fine — so needs_master_override is FALSE. (2) The face is a pure void (#0d0a18 ellipse), no skull, no bones to name — so the robed-death-grows-flesh trap has no bone anchor to lean on here. Repeat "the inside of the hood a solid pool of black shadow" in EVERY clip prompt, especially the rear-facing walk and death, or WAN will grow a human head and face inside the hood on the rear falls. (3) Front-facing robed walk zooms at the camera — reuse the lich v3 glide text / v5 march fix and the BEAM_CYCLE_WALK wrap-jump gate (ship <= 0.9x step) rather than re-deriving it.

RANGED UNIT: it is a ranged magic caster, so if the advance-while-firing behaviour ever gets a walk_attack strip beyond the archer, this unit is a candidate — not needed for this conversion.

So the DESC is already a pure rest identity. Do not let a later edit put the orbs into hands.

The shared master's "both legs and both feet" clause WILL grow a biped here — the override is mandatory, and it names "three" twice on purpose. Watch the walk clips: a three-legged cycle has no real-world reference, so the generator will likely default to a two-leg waddle with the middle peg dragging. If a roll comes back with a clean rocking/trundling shuffle (moveSpeed is 45, "trundles slowly"), take it — do not chase a proper gait.

SUMMARY CORRECTION: the inventory pass says the drum is "wider than it is tall". The source rect is 18 wide x 17 tall — essentially square. "Squat barrel-drum" is the honest read; I did not write "wider than it is tall" because pushing that makes the generator flatten it into a pancake and lose the barrel.

NO MID-GESTURE TRANSLATION WAS NEEDED. The procedural pose is already a rest pose; the only animated parts are A.glow (muzzle bore + eye brighten) and the rising() steam motes. The muzzle charge belongs in the ATTACK clip: cannon flash/recoil, no swing, no lean — this thing has no arms.

Two consequences: (1) promote MUST run with --medoid for this unit (asymmetric-unit rule from the lich/archer sessions); (2) check the rear (ne/nw) clips — if the cannon reads on the wrong side relative to the engine's projectile origin, this is the archer's left-handed-weapon problem again and wants a FACING_SWAP entry in export_to_game.py rather than a prompt fix. SILHOUETTE FILL. Drum + 12-unit cannon spur makes a wide, low silhouette. Expect a high FILL score; if the still gates at >=0.85, pad before the death clip or the collapse will crop at the cell edge.

PALETTE COLLISION. The Engineer (already in the shipped DESC table) is also "riveted brass" dwarven tech. Also note the sibling Deep Forge roster (clockwork_spider, animated_armor, forge_golem, ancient_automaton) is unconverted, so there is no existing construct body to reuse or recolour for this unit.

ONE EYE. The source draws a single amber disc at (-1,-3) with a cream #fff6d0 highlight. Both the DESC and the master say "single"/"one big" — if a roll comes back two-eyed, re-roll rather than edit; edits on faces have historically added the second eye back.

units.notes:
SOURCE READ IN FULL: sprites.ts:8821-8973 (drawForgeGolem), dispatch at :694 applies ctx.scale(1.25); UnitDef at data/units.ts:1427 (color #7c2d12 fire-blackened iron -> body/dark/light, accent #f97316 molten glow; battleScale 2.1, rarity epic, Deep Forge boss).

Two things it omitted that I put in the DESC: (a) the pair of ember-belching shoulder VENTS (dark slots at y=-13 with glowing accent bars), (b) the fists are two-part — boxy 8x16 forearm blocks plus dark knuckle-balls at the bottom with a molten knuckle-glow stripe. NO MID-GESTURE TRANSLATION WAS NEEDED. Unusually for a boss, the procedural art is already a rest stance — feet planted, arms hanging forward, nothing swung. I wrote "furnace opening" rather than "furnace maw"/"furnace mouth" deliberately to keep the word "mouth" off the prompt, and pinned it "set into its chest". If seeds come back with a glowing jaw and a dark chest, re-roll rather than edit; if it persists, try "a glowing arched grated hatch in the middle of its chest".

RISK 2 — THE TINY HEAD WILL WANT TO GROW. The source head is a ~10x7 wedge sunk between the shoulders with no neck; models normalise to human head-to-body ratio. "tiny sunken wedge head between the shoulders" is doing all the work. Judge this on the FRONT still before promoting — once the head is human-scale, every clip inherits it. RISK 3 — BOSS SCALE / DEATH-CLIP CLIPPING. This is a 2.1x boss with a 1.25x sprite multiplier and its widest points are the two fists at x=±22 (i.e. 44 units wide vs a 32-unit torso). It will fill the 80px cell fast. Apply the known rule: check the seed still's FILL before generating; if >=0.85, pad BEFORE the death clip, not after (and do not re-pad a promoted frame). Wide-armed falls are exactly the case that crops.

RISK 4 — NOT A RECOLOUR, BUT A NEIGHBOUR. rune_golem (sprites.ts:7389, the batch unit) is the same blocky-construct biped family: stone legs + blocky torso + glowing accents. Do NOT reuse rune_golem's converted body — the shapes diverge where it matters (forge_golem is HUNCHED with a trapezoid torso, long arms and forward-hanging fists; rune_golem is upright and compact) and the materials differ (carved stone + glyphs vs fire-blackened iron + furnace). If rune_golem's rig turned out well, reuse the RUNBOOK, not the art.

RISK 5 — GLOW BUDGET. The source has FOUR separate emissive zones (eyes, chest furnace, shoulder vents, knuckles) plus an engine-side pulsing heat aura and three rising-ember emitters. The aura and embers are Renderer liveliness, NOT sprite art — do not prompt them in, or the white background gets dirty and the matte suffers. The themed-legendary-aura pass is where the heat halo belongs.

ORIENTATION: check the se/ne walk for the classic construct failure — stubby 9x12 legs plus a heavy torso tends to produce a glide instead of a march. Apply the approved taste anchor (real march, wrap-jump <= 0.9x step size) and expect to iterate; the lich precedent says the cloth/mass damping is the fix, not adding bounce.

WORD COUNT: 70 words, starts lowercase with an article, no trailing period, one flowing phrase, rest pose only. Rules checked: R1 clean (no action verbs; "hanging" is a static state), R2 clean (zero negations), R3 clean ("shoulders", never "back"), R4 clean (no draw/aim/spin/turn), R5 material named twice ("forged of fire-blackened iron", "boxy iron fists"), R6 all colours as plain adjectives.

units.notes:
SOURCE READ IN FULL: C:/Users/Justin/Documents/fantasy-arena/src/assets/sprites.ts lines 8975-9068 (drawAncientAutomaton), dispatch at line 698 (`ctx.scale(1.15)`), UnitDef at C:/Users/Justin/Documents/fantasy-arena/src/data/units.ts:1458 (color "#78350f" ancient bronze = body, accent "#fde68a" pale gold filigree; sprites.ts:402-405 derives dark = shade(body,-45), light = shade(body,+40), so plates are a vertical light-bronze-to-dark-bronze gradient). Spine/gem/sparks are a separate warmer gold "#ffd86b" with white-gold "#fffbe6" hot points — I wrote that as "glowing gold" and "bright gold core gem" rather than two near-identical gold words.

INVENTORY SUMMARY IS ACCURATE, with three corrections from the source: (1) the energy spine runs from y=-16 to y=+12 only — head to waist — so NOTHING glows below the pelvis (the comment at 8988 says this was a deliberate fix). Do not let a clip grow a light bar between the shin plates. (2) The breastplate is a broad TRAPEZOID (22 wide at the top tapering to 16), not a hexagon. (3) The gold filigree is literally one horizontal line across the chest plus the diamond core gem — keep it sparse; heavy engraved filigree will muddy the 45px read.

NO MID-GESTURE TRANSLATION WAS NEEDED — this is the rare unit whose procedural art is already a rest pose. Its only motion is a hover bob (sin, ±1.4px), a slow breathing drift where every plate floats further out and back in (the `s` term), a core-gem pulse, and three sparks rising up the spine. That drift IS the idle: tell the animator to spend the idle clip on plates breathing apart/together plus the bob, not on a sway.

NO ARMS, NO HANDS, NO WEAPON exist in the source — the pauldrons float free at the shoulders with nothing between them and the waist. This is the main generation risk: WAN will want to hang arms off those pauldrons and put something in them. If a seed grows arms, reject it; if arms survive the gate, at least keep the hands empty. The ATTACK clip therefore has no swing to inherit — suggest the shoulder plates driving forward and the core gem flaring (it is a MELEE unit, range MELEE, attackSpeed 1.8 = slow heavy hits).

SILHOUETTE RISK / seed-still FILL: the identity is the GAPS. At sprite scale the plates will want to fuse into an ordinary solid armoured knight, which would make it a near-duplicate of the already-converted knight and of the unconverted `animated_armor`. Gate hard on "can you see white background through the gaps between helm, chest, waist and shins" before promoting a still. Judge the helm for a face too — the master's usual "face reads clearly" clause is why I wrote an override; the helm is a blank angular plate with two tiny gold slits, and a nose/mouth means re-roll.

DEATH CLIP: the sim's corpse decal for this unit is "rune_rubble" (src/assets/corpseArt.ts:68), so death should be the beam cutting out and the plates dropping/scattering into a heap, not a body falling. Because it hovers, the death has real vertical travel — pad the death clip generously.

SCALE: rendered at ctx.scale(1.15) in game (a "relic construct" bump) but it has NO `battleScale` field, so it is NOT a boss — standard 80px cell, no boss-scale handling needed. It is a rare-spawn quest catalyst (Deep Forge floor 5, `DEEP_FORGE_QUEST`) despite being rarity "legendary" in the data, and sprites.ts:5911-5913 lists it as one of the rare catalysts that gets a SIGNATURE themed aura — that pairs with the themed-legendary-aura work still open in the handoff; the aura here should be gold spine-sparks rising, matching the existing draw code.

BODY PLAN CALL: I filed it hovering-legless. It does have two narrow shin plates in the leg position, but they float, taper, and end flat at y=24 with no feet and no ground contact — running the shared master ("both legs and both feet planted on the ground") will grow feet, fuse the plates and delete the hover. Confidence is medium only because of that borderline call: if the override's "hovering just above the ground" makes the generator draw it too high in the frame (a levitating trophy rather than a fighter), tighten "just above the ground" to "a hand's width above the ground" rather than reverting to the shared master.

units.notes:
MID-GESTURE TRANSLATION PERFORMED. The procedural art (drawHereticZealot, src/assets/sprites.ts:9077-9147) draws the arm thrust forward brandishing the knife, with a live `jab` oscillation (Math.max(0, sin(t*5))*2) pushing the blade further out each frame. I rested it: "one hand holding a short curved pale-steel knife low at its side". The forward jab belongs in the ATTACK clip — tell the attack prompt to thrust the knife forward and slightly up (the procedural blade tip travels from roughly (14,-7) to (19,-14), i.e. an upward-outward stab, not a downward chop).

SUMMARY WAS INCOMPLETE — I added the chest sigil. The inventory pass missed a real prop: a heretic sun-mark daubed on the chest in the accent colour (accent #f6c453 at 0.85 alpha) — an arc'd circle of radius ~3.4 with a diagonal line struck through it. It sits dead centre on the robe and is the unit's only mid-body read, so it is in the DESC. It is small; at 45px it will read as a gold smudge — do NOT fail a seed for losing its exact geometry, only for losing the gold chest accent entirely.

PALETTE IS FROM THE UNITDEF (src/data/units.ts:1491-1505): color #4c1d24 "wine-dark vestments", accent #f6c453 "guttering candle gold". The robe is a vertical gradient light->body->dark (withShade +40 / -45, sprites.ts:403-404), so a lit top and near-black lower hem is correct and on-model. Hardcoded extras: rope belt #a08a5a (tan), knife blade #cfc8bd (pale grey steel), hood interior #0c0810 (near-black).

HOOD-VOID RISK — this is the necromancer/lich failure mode. The face is a filled black ellipse with two glowing dots. WAN will try to grow a human face inside the hood on any clip where the head turns, and especially on rear-facing falls/deaths. Mitigation is positive restatement, never negation: keep "the hood swallowing the head into black emptiness lit by two glowing candle-gold eye-glints" verbatim in per-clip prompts. If a rear clip still grows a face, re-roll the seed rather than editing the prompt (mid-gesture-DESC-means-re-roll-the-MASTER precedent).

MATERIAL: this one is genuinely a mortal human under cloth, so "cloth" carries it — but do not drop the word. An unnamed material on a hooded silhouette drifts toward a generic bare-armed brawler.

BODY REUSE: this is the robed-humanoid plan (lich, necromancer, eclipse_acolyte family). If the necromancer is already converted, its walk/death timing and the damped-cloth calibration should transfer — but do NOT reuse its art: this one is shorter, hunched, wine-dark rather than violet, and its hem is a torn sawtooth of triangular points rather than a smooth sweep. The sawtooth hem is the main silhouette tell at 45px; if a seed smooths it into a plain skirt, reject.

SHARED MASTER IS CORRECT (no override). Robe apex y=-13, hem y=16..20 — the robe reaches the ground and the shared master's "both feet planted" clause will put feet under the hem. That matches the shipped lich/necromancer precedent and is acceptable.

SCALE: no ctx.scale() at the dispatch site (sprites.ts:704-706) — this renders at 1.0, and it has no `battleScale` in the UnitDef. No boss-scale padding needed.

DEATH-CLIP PADDING: the jagged hem plus the outstretched knife makes this wider than its robe suggests. Run the seed-still FILL check before committing the death clip — if FILL >= 0.85, pad, or the knife arm and hem points clip on the fall.

HANDEDNESS: the procedural arm is drawn at positive x (viewer's right). I listed the knife as right-hand for the pipeline, but the DESC deliberately says "one hand" and leaves it free — a melee knife has no engine-side aim vector, so the archer's FACING_SWAP problem does NOT apply here. Whichever hand the winning seed picks, keep it consistent across all eight clips.

WALK: the rope belt's loose end sways in the procedural art (sin(t*2.4)). That is a nice motion cue for the walk clip but it is cloth — apply the damped-cloth taste anchor (wrap-jump <= 0.9x step size) so it does not become the drift the way the lich's cloak did.

units.master_override: A hi-bit pixel art game sprite of {desc}, hovering in the air above the ground, facing the viewer, its full body visible with its two clawed legs hanging loosely in the air clear of the ground beneath it and its wings spread wide to either side. Seen from a high three-quarter overhead camera tilted about 60 degrees down toward the ground — the classic camera of a top-down 16-bit action role-playing game — so the tops of the spread wings and the top of the horned head read from above while the face and chest still read clearly. Flat even overhead light, identical on the left and right, no cast shadow. Exactly one creature, centred, drawn small with wide empty margin on all sides, on a plain pure white background.
units.notes:
SOURCE READ IN FULL: dispatch at sprites.ts:707 -> drawGargoyle at sprites.ts:9292, plus helpers gargoyleWings (9177) and gargoyleHead (9205). UnitDef at units.ts:1506 (color #57534e weathered granite, accent #a8a29e pale stone grey; rare, melee, Fallen Cathedral tier).

TWO VARIANTS MERGED INTO ONE STRIP (per the brief). Variant 0 "Stone Imp": round 7.5r granite torso, chest cracks, wing span 21, straight upright horn pair (swept:false), eyes #cdd2d8 (pale cold grey-white), tail off the LEFT. Variant 1 "Ravager": broader 9.2x8 slab chest with pectoral seams, wing span 24, swept-curved horns PLUS a second inner straight horn pair, eyes #9db8ff (cold periwinkle), tail off the RIGHT, orbiting stone chips. I wrote the straight upright horn pair (the human's flagged call) and split the eye colour to "pale ice-blue", which serves both #cdd2d8 and #9db8ff. Chest is written as "rounded barrel" — reads as either the stocky or the slab body at 45px.

MID-GESTURE TRANSLATIONS I PERFORMED (three):
1. The procedural art is a LIVE HOVER — sin-driven bob, wing flap (flapAmp 3.5 / 5), leg drift, tail sway. All of that is animation, not identity. The DESC pins the wings to a rest "spread to either side" and the legs to "hanging loosely". The flap belongs in the walk/idle clips — do NOT let it into the DESC or the master.
2. gargoyleHead draws a permanent snarl arc + fangs. I kept "snarl" only as a NOUN attached to "carved" ("a carved snarl showing ivory-white fangs") so it reads as chiselled stonework, not a mid-roar action. If a still comes back mid-roar, weaken to "a fanged stone muzzle" rather than deleting the fangs — the fangs are the only warm colour on the whole unit (#e9e4d4) and carry the read.
3. Particle FX are omitted on purpose: variant 0 sheds falling stone dust, variant 1 orbits 3 stone chips. Neither is silhouette; asking for them costs pixels and invites floating debris that the pipeline reads as a second object ("exactly one creature").

MASTER OVERRIDE IS MANDATORY HERE — read this before generating. This unit HAS two legs but they NEVER touch the ground: both branches translate the whole body up by -7 / -6 before drawing, and the legs terminate in dangling talons at y=16..19 with a hover drift. The shared master's "both legs and both feet planted on the ground" would ground it and destroy the entire identity (the unit's role is literally "Waking Stone" airborne fodder; the human's inventory note flagged HOVERS in caps). body_plan is set to "winged-humanoid" and NOT "hovering-legless" because the dangling clawed legs are a real silhouette element that must survive — if you map body_plan straight to a template, override that mapping and use the master_override text above.

RISKS FOR THE CLIP GENERATOR:
- FILL / death padding: the wing span is 21-24 units against a ~7.5-9 torso, i.e. the sprite is roughly 3x wider than tall in the shoulders. Expect a high seed-still FILL (>=0.85 likely). Per the known trap, PAD THE DEATH CLIP or the wingtips will clip on the rear-facing falls.
- Rear (ne/nw) death is the flesh-risk clip. Material is named four ways (stone gargoyle / carved from weathered grey granite / bat-style stone wings / pricked stone ears) precisely so WAN cannot grow skin on the rear falls. If a rear clip still comes back fleshy, add "solid carved stone all over" rather than any negation.
- Mild asymmetry: the tail hangs off one side (left in v0, right in v1). Mirroring for the nw/sw directions will flip the tail, which is harmless. The rest of the body is symmetric, so a medoid promote is NOT required here (unlike the asymmetric weapon units).
- No held props at all — nothing goes in GEAR, and no hand should acquire a weapon. If a seed hands it a spear or club, re-roll rather than edit.
- Scale: the dispatch applies NO ctx.scale to the gargoyle (unlike fallen_seraph 1.25x / penitent 1.1x in the same tier), so there is no boss-scale concern — generate it at ordinary fodder scale.
- Wing construction is explicitly "drawImp's construction, scaled" (see the gargoyleWings docstring). If the imp is ever converted, match the membrane read between the two so the Cathedral flock and the Vault imps stay in the same visual family.
- Walk clip: this thing does not walk — it flies, and its game moveSpeed is 45 ("grinding stone gait"). Suggest the walk template be a slow forward hover-drift with a wing beat and the legs trailing, NOT a leg cycle. Attack template: it is melee (range MELEE, attackSpeed 1.8, i.e. slow heavy hits) — a diving claw rake or a heavy two-fisted stone smash on the beat, with the tail whipping as follow-through.

Seen from a high three-quarter overhead camera tilted about 60 degrees down toward the ground — the classic camera of a top-down 16-bit action role-playing game — so the top of its hood and the tops of its shoulders read from above while its face and the front of the shroud still read clearly. units.notes:
SOURCE: drawGraveChorister, src/assets/sprites.ts:9455-9529; UnitDef src/data/units.ts:1521-1536 (color #8b93a7 "faded shroud grey" = body, dark = -45 shade, light = +40 shade, accent #dbe4ff "spectral glow"). Body path spans x -9..9, y -17 (cinched crown) to y 20, with a three-point jagged hem (notches at y13, points at y19/20/19) and the fill gradient fading to 15% alpha at the bottom — that is why the desc says "thinning ... into a ragged wisp-tapered hem" rather than a robe hem. Hood is body shaded -18 (slightly darker grey than the shroud). Face is near-black #10131d: two 1.3x1.8 eye ellipses + one tall 1.7x2.6 mouth ellipse — the face IS the read at 45px, so keep it in any trimmed variant.

MID-GESTURE TRANSLATIONS I MADE (2):
1. The wail. In-game the mouth ellipse pulses (Math.sin(A.t*3.2)) and three accent-coloured arcs ripple outward from it — the unit is literally drawn mid-song. I translated that to the static shape only: "one long oval mouth hanging open". I deliberately avoided the word "wailing"/"singing" (adjacent to the banned "snarling mid-roar") and I LEFT THE SOUND RINGS OUT of the DESC — concentric arcs in front of the face at 80px will either eat the mouth or read as a collar. Put the rings in the ATTACK clip prompt, not here.
2. The candle. There are no arms in the source at all — the wax stick (#d8cfb8) and its gold flame (#ffd76a, shadowBlur glow) are drawn free-floating at x -8.5, just outside the shroud silhouette. I pinned it as "a cream wax candle with a warm gold flame at one side" and set GEAR hand=floating. Expect the generator to grow a cloth hand or sleeve around it; that is acceptable and probably reads better. Do NOT write "holding" — a hand implies an arm implies human flesh on this unit.

BODY-PLAN RISK (this is the FLOATING/HOVERING case with no precedent):
- Master override is mandatory. The shared master's "both legs and both feet planted on the ground" will grow legs under the hem; the source has none (comment in sprites.ts:9484 literally reads "no legs — it drifts", and there is a sin-bob hover offset).
- The lich lesson applies inverted: robed-undead grows FLESH on rear falls unless the material is named. Here there are no bones to name — the answer is CLOTH. If a rear-facing death or walk clip grows a face, hands or bare limbs, re-roll with "empty spectral shroud-cloth with nothing inside it" (positive phrasing) rather than adding a negation.
- DEATH CLIP: a hovering unit cannot "fall over" convincingly and the sim gives it no ground contact. Ask for a sink-and-dissipate (shroud collapsing downward into shreds), not a topple. Flag this first in the batch review notes. corpseArt.ts:61 maps it to "robes" remains, so the collapsed decal is a cloth heap — the death should end low and flat to match.
- FILL/PADDING: the silhouette is tall and narrow (37 tall x 18 wide in sprite units) and the arcs/glow push the effective bounds wider. Watch the seed FILL number; if >=0.85 pad the death clip before generating.

OTHER: bespoke body — this is NOT a recolour of any converted unit (the Cathedral tier's penitent/fallen_seraph reuse healer/angelWing bodies, this one does not). Rare-tier monster, no battleScale, so normal 80px cell, no boss-scale concern. Facing: only the candle is asymmetric (drawn on the -x side), so the mirror is safe; no FACING_SWAP needed (unlike the archer). Unverified: I did not generate anything — this is the text only.

units.notes:
SOURCE READ IN FULL. Dispatch: sprites.ts:715 `case "fallen_seraph"` -> `ctx.scale(1.25)` -> drawFallenSeraph (sprites.ts:9550), which is only three things: angelWing(-1/+1, beat 0.28, sc 1.28, tone "ashen") (sprites.ts:3805), four ember motes shed from the wing roots (#e8843c, A.live only), and a tilted broken halo (accent stroke, rotate 0.5 rad, ellipse arc 0.5 -> 2PI-0.35 so a bite is missing from the ring). The ENTIRE body is then drawHealer (sprites.ts:3623). UnitDef (units.ts:1540): name "Seraphiel the Forsworn", epic, battleScale 2.1, color #3f3348 ("ash-stained ivory gone dusk"), accent #e8b04b ("tarnished halo gold"); body/dark/light derive at sprites.ts:402-404 so the face is withShade(light,-10) = pale ashen lilac, NOT skin tone.

SIBLING WARNING (the summary's flag is real and load-bearing): `penitent` (drawPenitent, sprites.ts:9531) is the SAME call stack — ashen angelWing at sc 1.1 with a drooped beat, a dim INTACT halo, then drawHealer — differing only in palette (color #cfc6b8 ash-dusted alabaster, accent #9db8ff cold votive blue) and scale (1.1). The deckable Cleric (`healer`) is the same body again in white/gold. Convert this one first and the penitent is a palette + wing-angle + halo edit of the same body; they MUST read as siblings, so do not let a re-roll drift this one into a different silhouette.

MID-GESTURE: none to translate. The crozier is already vertical in source (shaft y=18 -> y=-11 at x=+12, crook arc + orb at y=-15), and the wings flap around a RAISED rest angle (beat = 0.28 + a +/-0.12 sine), i.e. fanned wide at rest. I still pinned "upright at one side like a walking staff" as a cheap staff guard.

DOUBLE HALO: drawHealer paints its own upright double gold halo at y=-23 IN ADDITION to the boss's tilted broken ring at y=-21, so the in-game sprite actually shows two overlapping halos. I collapsed that to one broken askew halo — the identity read the human flagged, and two rings is mush at 45px. If a reviewer says the halo looks wrong versus the game, this is why.

BOSS-SCALE / FILL RISK: 2.1x battleScale plus the sprite's own 1.25x, and the fanned wingspan is the widest thing in the roster so far (angelWing primaries ~27 units each side at sc 1.28; footprint rx 22 vs the usual ~14). Expect the still to FILL the cell. Check the seed FILL metric before promoting — >=0.85 means pad the death clip, and run every clip with --pad; wingtips will clip on attack and on the rear falls otherwise.

PARTICLES: the ember motes and the halo spark are A.live-only, so the static procedural sprite has neither. I deliberately wrote the embers as attached root glow ("glowing ember-orange at their roots") rather than floating motes — detached particles inflate the alpha bbox and wreck the crop.

REAR-CLIP FLESH TRAP: this is the lich/necromancer lesson again, worse here because wings occlude the torso from the rear. "charred soot-grey feathered" + "ash-pale" + "ash-stained cloth" are the material anchors; if a rear fall grows pink flesh or bare human shoulders, re-roll rather than edit.

DETAILS DROPPED FOR THE 75-WORD BUDGET, in case a review calls them out: dark skullcap over the crown, serene closed eyes stroked in brown (I kept "closed eyes" but lost "serene"), shoulder mantle, tan rope belt with knot and hanging cord, prayer book on the screen-left hip with a gold cross, gold backlight halo-glow behind the whole figure. Add the skullcap back ("under a dark cap") if the generated head reads bare.

PALETTE NOTE: the summary called the wings "ashen-grey"; the source gradient is darker than that (primaries #57505c -> #2a252f, with warmer ashen-brown coverts #8a6f6a/#4d3c3a), so I wrote "charred soot-grey". If the human wants them lighter to match the summary wording, swap to "charred ash-grey" — one-word change, no other effect.

MASTER: false — robed biped, hem reaches the ground with no feet drawn (robe triangle ends y=20), which is exactly the lich/necromancer precedent where the shared master works and the generator invents feet under the hem. Corpse decal for this unit is "feathers" (corpseArt.ts:132), so steer the death clip toward a scatter of charred feathers if the exporter lets you.

units.notes:
SOURCE READ IN FULL. `drawPenitent` (sprites.ts:9531) is only three things: two `angelWing(..., "ashen")` at scale 1.1 with beat -0.08 (drooped), a dim guttering accent ellipse halo at y=-21, then a straight call to `drawHealer` (sprites.ts:3623). def: color #cfc6b8 (ash-dusted alabaster), accent #9db8ff (cold votive light), rarity LEGENDARY (the code comment calling it "a grand, grieving rare" is stale), no battleScale.

BODY IS A REUSE, NOT A NEW BUILD. `drawHealer` is the exact same function `healer` (Cleric) and `priest` already use — both already have DESC entries in pixel_fa.py. I deliberately mirrored the shipped `priest` phrasing (tabard/cross, skullcap, closed eyes, rope belt, crozier) so the family reads as one; if the priest's converted sprite landed well, reuse its scaffolding and just re-tint + add wings.

SIBLING WARNING (the summary's flag, confirmed in source): `fallen_seraph` calls the SAME `angelWing(..., "ashen")` + `drawHealer`, only with a bigger span (1.28 vs 1.1), a raised beat, ember motes, a broken halo, color #3f3348 dusk-violet and accent #e8b04b tarnished gold — and battleScale 2.1. Build the wings ONCE and share them; differ only in tone (this one pale ash + cold blue, the seraph dusk-purple + tarnished gold). The Penitent itself has NO battleScale, so no boss-scale padding here — the fallen_seraph will need it.

BIGGEST DRIFT RISK: the accent is COLD PALE BLUE, not gold. Halo, tabard cross and crozier orb are all #9db8ff. Every other angel/cleric in the roster (priest, healer, seraph) is warm gold, and the generator will want to pull this one gold too — check the pick for that first.

MID-GESTURE: none to translate. The procedural pose is already a rest stance (crozier vertical from y=18 to y=-11, hands not extended). The wing flap and halo gutter are live-animation only and are stated as a static droop in the DESC. The attack clip should be a raised-crozier cast, not a swing — it is a ranged healer (range 0.3x field, flash_heal + renew).

HALO COMPRESSION: the sprite actually stacks THREE rings — the dim guttering ellipse from drawPenitent plus drawHealer's radiant double halo (7.5 and 4.5 radius at y=-23). I compressed to one dim pale-blue halo; a ring stack reads as noise at 45px. Add "two thin halo rings" like the priest entry only if the pick looks bare.

EYEBALL THIS: (1) rear-facing (ne/nw) walk + death — wide wings on a robed body, the lich lesson says robed-death-must-name-the-bones or WAN grows flesh; here "charred ... feathered" is the material anchor, verify it holds on rear falls. (2) FILL on the seed — the wingspan is wide, expect >=0.85, pad the death clip. (3) The prayer book hangs at the hip OPPOSITE the crozier (x=-13 vs x=+12) and is ~6px; drift/loss on side and rear views is an acceptable FAIL. (4) Closed downcast eyes — generators love to open them. (5) Hand side: the procedural crozier sits on the VIEWER's right, i.e. the character's left hand — the DESC says only "in one hand" on purpose, but if the rear draws end up mirrored the archer's FACING_SWAP precedent applies.

units.notes:
SOURCE vs SUMMARY CONFLICT — trust the source: the inventory pass says "hooded jerkin", but drawCutpurse (sprites.ts:9778-9911) has NO hood. Its own header comment reads "Deliberately NOT the Outlaw's hooded-duelist silhouette (no deep hood, no twin daggers)" and the head block is literally "bare head: burglar mask + messy hair" — skin #c99b6a circle, near-black band #1c1a22 across the eyes, messy dark-brown #3a2c22 tufts, two gold accent eye-dots. The bare masked head IS the identity at 45px. If a seed grows a hood, reject it — the Den already ships three other hooded/rogue silhouettes (knife_thrower, silencer, bandit_king) and this one is the odd man out on purpose.

MID-GESTURE TRANSLATIONS I PERFORMED (procedural art is a full sprint getaway):
1. ctx.rotate(-0.13) "leaning into the sprint" -> upright rest stance.
2. Scarf is stroked as two streaming ribbons trailing 13-15px behind the neck -> "knotted at his throat"; ends now just hang. Do NOT let a seed re-stream it horizontally, that reads as running.
3. Shiv drawn at x=+11.5 "out front" on an extended arm -> "held low at his side". The lunge belongs in the ATTACK clip.
4. The five spinning gold coins arcing away behind him are `A.live`-only spill VFX (spinCoin loop) — dropped from the DESC entirely. The coins that survive are the static glints at the sack mouth and on the belt purse.

SCALE: the dispatch does `ctx.scale(0.9, 0.9)` with the comment "a small, quick gutter blade" — this unit is deliberately UNDERSIZED. Keep it small and compact in the cell; do not let the generator give it heroic 8-head proportions. Reject any seed that reads tall or broad-shouldered.

INVENTED DETAIL (flagging honestly): the procedural body draws no legs at all — the jerkin is one roundRect(-6.5,-4,13,21,5) that just rounds off at the bottom under the capelet. "dark leggings and soft leather boots" is my addition, required because the shared master demands both feet planted. Nothing in the source contradicts it; the palette is already near-black at the belt (#20161a).

PALETTE from data/units.ts:1610 — color #3b3347 (dusk grey-violet, the jerkin/capelet; capelet is withShade(body,-26) so slightly darker than the torso), accent #d4a017 (stolen-coin gold: both eyes, the purse glint, the sack glints). Non-def hardcodes: scarf #c94f3d (brick/rust red), sack + purse #4a3a28 (worn brown leather), shiv blade #cfd3da pale steel with a #2a1d12 dark wrapped grip, skin #c99b6a, hair #3a2c22.

TWO-PROP / HANDEDNESS: sack under the LEFT arm, shiv in the RIGHT hand (source comments say exactly that), so this needs a GEAR pin. Right-handed blade is the good case — the archer's FACING_SWAP hack in export_to_game.py was needed because its weapon was LEFT-handed and rear draws aimed wrong; nothing here should need that, but check the ne/nw frames keep the sack on the same side as the se/sw ones or the silhouette flips mid-turn.

NOT a recolour of any converted body — no previously converted unit is a small unhooded masked human. Closest converted neighbour is the archer (lean leather biped), but the compact height, capelet and hugged sack are different enough that it needs its own rolls.

BOSS SCALE: none, this is fodder (rare-tier Den mob, hp 42). Compact body means low FILL, so death clips should have room; still check the seed-still FILL before promoting, and pad if >=0.85.

EYEBALL THIS: (a) no hood; (b) the mask band reads as a band across the eyes, not a full balaclava or a blindfold; (c) the sack stays a sack and does not merge into the capelet blob at 80px; (d) the shiv stays short — it is 8 units of blade, a shiv, not a dagger or shortsword; (e) the scarf stays red and does not turn into a hood liner.

units.notes:
SOURCE: dispatch `case "knife_thrower"` at src/assets/sprites.ts:729 -> drawKnifeThrower (sprites.ts:9614-9732, helper `heldKnife` at 9594). UnitDef at src/data/units.ts:1624-1648 — color #2c333d "oiled leather", accent #84cc16 "venom lime"; role "Poisoned Steel"; ranged (0.28 field width), poison rider every 3rd shot. Renderer derives dark = color-45 (near-black charcoal) and light = color+40 (slate grey), so the jerkin is a slate-grey-to-near-black gradient — "oiled near-black leather" is the honest read, not pure black.

MID-GESTURE TRANSLATION PERFORMED (the summary's ** warning, confirmed in source): the procedural pose is a full throw — torso pitched forward (ctx.rotate(-0.14)), lunge legs (rear leg extended, front bent), LEAD arm fully extended in follow-through with an OPEN EMPTY hand, the OFF-hand pulling the next blade off the bandolier on a 1.2s cycle, and a separate spinning knife streaking away with a venom-lime glint trail. I translated to: planted stance, one knife at rest point-up in one hand, other hand low. The follow-through arm, the streaking spinning knife and the lime trail are ATTACK-clip material — do not let them into the idle.

RESIDUAL RISK: the phrase "throwing knife" is the one gerund left in the DESC (it is the object's name, and dropping it costs the identity). If the master seeds come back mid-throw, RE-ROLL THE MASTER — do not try to edit the gesture out (mid-gesture edits either keep the gesture or delete the knife; the archer proved this).

NOT a recolour of any converted unit — no rogue-family body exists in the pipeline yet (the 12 done are ogre/knight/orc/skeleton/archer/lich/necromancer/fire_mage/seraph/wolf/slime/rune_golem). It is deliberately distinct from the pixel_fa.py "rogue"/"outlaw"/"trickster" entries (those are hooded, faces in shadow, dagger in each hand); this one is BARE-HEADED with cropped hair, visible eyes, and a red lower-face mask. The crimson mask is shared on purpose with the bandit_king in the game art — whoever writes the bandit_king DESC should reuse this exact mask wording so the Den reads as one gang.

Scale: no `battleScale` on the def and no ctx.scale in the dispatch case, so it renders at 1.0 (the cutpurse is 0.9, the bandit_king 1.25) — normal 80px cell, no boss-scale padding needed.

Palette liberty to watch: venom-lime (#84cc16) appears in the procedural art ONLY as the thrown knife's glint trail (plus the poison rider colour) — I promoted it to "edge slick with venom-green poison" so the DESC carries the unit's accent at 45px. If takes come back reading too green (a green blade instead of steel), cut that clause and let the accent live in the attack VFX instead.

Small details deliberately dropped for word budget: pale-steel spare knife glints (#cfd6dd) on the bandolier strap, the darker crimson seam stitched across the mask, the fluttering animation on the mask tails. Keep the tails as static hanging cloth in the idle and let the walk/attack clips move them — cloth that flutters in the master tends to grow into a scarf or cape.

Death clip: corpseArt maps this unit to "loot_spill" (src/assets/corpseArt.ts:123), so a compact death that ends with the body low and knives scattered matches the in-game decal.

units.notes:
MID-GESTURE TRANSLATION PERFORMED. The procedural art (drawDenBruiser, src/assets/sprites.ts:9915-10085) thrusts BOTH huge open hands forward at chest height with the fingers flexing — a grapple/lock-up pose. I translated that to "huge bare open hands hanging low at his sides" so the master's rest stance holds. The grapple belongs in the ATTACK clip: he is a pure unarmed melee unit (range: MELEE, ability "lifesteal" = passive filler, never casts), so the attack should be a two-handed grab/shove or a haymaker, never a weapon swing.

WATCH: no weapon anywhere in the source. A weaponless brawler is exactly the case where the generator likes to invent a club or an axe into an empty hand — eyeball the base still and every attack frame for a hallucinated prop. If one appears, re-roll rather than editing it out (an edit at cfg=1 either keeps the gesture or deletes the hands).

DATA/ART DISAGREEMENT: units.ts:1661 comments accent #c98a3d as "brass knuckles", but the draw function never draws knuckles — accent is spent on the glowing chest pit-brand, the belt buckle and the (animation-only) stomp ring. I trusted the source: hands are bare. Do not add knuckles.

ANIMATION-ONLY, deliberately excluded from the DESC: dust puffs at the feet, a stomp shockwave ellipse every 2.2s, the 2.0Hz torso "heave" breathing, the finger flex, and the pit-brand's glow pulse (0.4-0.9 alpha live, 0.6 static). The brand should read as a steady soft brass glow in the base still — it is a small ring with a slash through it, roughly 2px radius on a 40px-tall body, so expect the generator to enlarge it; that is acceptable as long as it stays a single small chest sigil and does not become a full breastplate emblem.

SILHOUETTE RISK — OGRE BLEED. This is a squat, top-heavy, wide-stanced, bald bruiser, which is proportionally close to the already-converted OGRE. The DESC says "human" and "tan skin" for exactly this reason; if the rolls come back with green/grey skin, tusks or a hunched-over-knuckles ape stance, that is ogre contamination — re-roll, do not accept. He is NOT a recolour of the ogre body and should not be built from ogre frames. (The unit was explicitly rewritten away from a shrunken-ogre recolour — see the comment at sprites.ts:9913.)

Rare tier, no battleScale field, so he renders at normal rank-and-file size — no boss-scale padding needed. Corpse decal is "loot_spill", so a compact death that settles near the feet reads best; the wide leg stance means his standing FILL is already broad, so check the seed's fill before committing the death clip (>=0.85 => pad).

Body plan is an ordinary biped with visible legs and feet, so the SHARED master applies unchanged.

Palette check against the derived tints: body #5b4632 with dark = #2e1905 (near-black brown, used for the legs) and light = #836e5a (muted taupe-brown, the vest highlight). Bare skin is #b98a5e with #a5764b shading and a #8a5f3e brow; teeth are off-white #e9e4d4; eyes are near-black #14181d; belt is #2c2118. "Dark brown hide vest" + "bare scarred tan skin" covers all of it in plain words.

units.notes:
SOURCE READ: dispatch at src/assets/sprites.ts:735 (`case "bandit_king"` applies an extra ctx.scale(1.25) before calling drawBanditKing at :10144); blade helper falchionAt at :10089. UnitDef at src/data/units.ts:1666 — color #4a2c3a "wine-stained leathers", accent #e8b04b "stolen crown gold", battleScale 2.1, epic boss of rogues_den (src/data/dungeons.ts:420).

MID-GESTURE TRANSLATIONS I PERFORMED (R1): (1) the procedural right arm runs a full 2.2s falchion swing — windup, chop, recover (sprites.ts:10258-10296) with a white/gold swoosh arc. I rested it: "the other holding a broad steel cleaver-falchion with a gold guard held down at his side". The chop belongs in the ATTACK clip and should be a big overhead cleave, shoulder-cocked then down through the arc. (2) Chest "heave" sine (:10146) and the fluttering mask knot-tails (:10358) are idle-only motion — do NOT bake either into the DESC still; they are free IDLE-clip material. (3) The left arm is ALREADY a rest pose in the source (fist planted on the hip with a gold bracer) so I kept it — it is the boss's silhouette read, but if a seed renders it as an arm crossed over the chest, re-roll rather than edit (GEAR_HOLD-rides-on-attack applies: an edit will either keep the gesture or delete the falchion).

DETAILS I DROPPED for the 35-75 word budget, in case a seed needs enriching: looped gold chains + a gold medallion across the coat (:10194-10217), a gold belt with a square buckle (:10218-10225), a brown coin-purse of spoils at the belt with a glinting gold coin (:10226-10236), the gold bracer band on the hip-fist forearm (:10248-10257), and the dark brown beard — which the mask covers almost entirely anyway (:10302-10308, comment says "mostly hidden under the mask now"). Crown + gold guard already carry the #e8b04b accent, so the sprite does not look under-golded without them. The "eyes" are literally two angry dark brow slashes ABOVE the mask line (:10367-10375) plus two small dark pupils under it — "hard eyes above it" is the honest translation; do not let a seed give him a visible mouth or a bare chin, the red cloth covers the whole lower face down to a point at the chin.

NOT A RECOLOUR of any already-converted body. Closest converted precedent is the OGRE (barrel-chested brute proportions, wide planted legs) — reuse its rear-fall/death handling instincts, but he is a fully clothed human, not a hulking monster, and his coat hem plus the crown change the overhead read.

BOSS-SCALE WARNING: battleScale 2.1 AND an extra 1.25x in the dispatch. The pixel cell is a fixed 80px, so he must still be drawn small with wide margin like every other unit — the game scales him up at draw time. Do not prompt him "huge" or "filling the frame". The wide crown spikes plus a broad falchion held out to one side make him a WIDE silhouette: expect FILL to run high, so check the seed-still FILL and if it is >=0.85, pad the death clip (seed-still-FILL-predicts-death-clipping) — the death is where the crown and the blade fly widest.

HAND SIDE: the procedural art seats the falchion on the screen-RIGHT side of the body (sx = +9). I deliberately left the DESC hand-agnostic ("one fist... the other holding...") so the generator picks; that avoids the left-handed-weapon-breaks-rear-aim trap. He is melee, so no engine arrow has to line up — but if the ne/nw clips come out with the blade on the wrong side of the body, FACING_SWAP in export_to_game.py is the fix, not a prompt edit.

FACE/MASK RISK on the rear-facing clips: a masked lower face is exactly the kind of thing WAN "fixes" by growing a normal human face when the body turns away. The DESC names the mask as cloth over the WHOLE lower face; if a rear fall still un-masks him, re-roll the seed rather than editing (edits on this unit tend to drop the crown).

units.notes:
SOURCE: `case "silencer"` (src/assets/sprites.ts:739) calls drawOutlaw(), which is a wrapper — it calls drawAssassin() (sprites.ts:3238) for the body and layers phantom after-image echoes on top (battlefield: 4 ghost copies trailing a sine phase-shift; hub portrait: a ghost peeling off sideways plus two red accent speed-lines). Tints come from UnitDef silencer (src/data/units.ts:1694): color "#1c1a22" (near-black, a hair violet), accent "#b91c1c" (crimson). dark = withShade(color,-45), so the hood/face are effectively black; face shadow is a hardcoded "#0d0912" ellipse. Confirmed strokes: darker tattered cape behind the body with a 4-point zigzag hem, slim gradient torso, diagonal chest sash, near-black belt + a small pouch on one hip, two pale forearm wraps, deep hood with a white rim light on one edge, two narrow accent-glow eye slits, twin daggers (drawDagger, sprites.ts:3340) angled blade-up-and-outward, dark handle, bronze crossguard "#6b532e", blade gradient steel-white at the guard to the ACCENT at the tip — hence "crimson-glowing blade" (do not let it render as a red-hot molten knife; it is steel with a red sheen).

MID-GESTURE TRANSLATIONS: minimal. The daggers are drawn angled outward with an animated venom drip; the DESC keeps them simply held in the hands, no stab/throw/lunge verb. The two live-only FX (red shadow-smoke wisps rising at the feet, and the phantom-flicker after-images) are deliberately OMITTED — they are presentation motion, and putting smoke in the DESC would fight the ground plane and the "exactly one character" clause. If we want them, they belong in the game-side themed-legendary aura pass, not the sprite.

REUSED BODY: this is the same draw function as outlaw / assassin / rogue / trickster (all four already have DESC entries). The silencer is the near-black + crimson member of that family; the outlaw is dusk-grey with brass-gold eyes. Do NOT ART_ALIAS the silencer onto outlaw strips — the palette is wrong (grey coat, gold eyes) and the red eyes are the whole read. Either roll it fresh, or run a palette-swap pass off the outlaw strips if one exists.

CHIRALITY / GEAR: twin identical daggers are symmetric, so no GEAR screen-side pin is needed (precedent: outlaw/assassin/rogue/trickster have no GEAR entry; GEAR is only populated for the shield+sword knights, skeleton, archer, orc). The real risk on the rear facings (n / ne) is the daggers vanishing rather than swapping sides — eyeball the rear stills and re-roll the seed instead of adding a GEAR clause.

READABILITY WARNING: a near-black body plus the pixelizer's 1px dark outline can collapse into a flat silhouette at 45px. The only anchors are the two red eye slits, the white hood rim light, and the steel dagger blades. Check the death and rear-facing frames specifically for the body reading as an undifferentiated black mass; if it flattens, lift the DESC from "near-black" to "very dark charcoal with a cold violet cast" and re-roll the master rather than patching downstream clips.

SCALE: despite being a legendary rare-spawn quest boss for The Rogue's Den, the def carries NO battleScale, so it renders at normal unit size — no boss-scale padding needed. Slim upright biped, low FILL/clipping risk on the death clip.

MASTER: shared MASTER is correct — ordinary hooded biped, same as the whole assassin family. No override.
